top_profane.text:
As bona fide "chileheads" and unrepentant "hopheads," my wife and I are always on the lookout for new culinary and craft beer experiences to satisfy our admittedly extreme tastes. We're huge fans of habañero chiles--jalapeños are far too mild--and even bigger fans of the amazing beers produced by the Stone Brewing Company of Escondido, California. One of Stone's beers that we most enjoy is a seasonally released strong ale called Double Bastard, an amped-up version of the flagship Arrogant Bastard ale that put Stone on the map for craft beer connoisseurs everywhere. At 11.2% ABV, Double Bastard is a strong, hoppy beer lover's dream come true. What could be better, then, than a hot pepper sauce that combines the eye-watering heat and distinctive flavor of habañero chiles with the rich body and mouth-puckering bitterness of Double Bastard? Well, the answer is easy--nothing could be better.

Double Bastard Double Burn Habañero Hot Sauce is distributed by Carlsbad Gourmet, a craft food purveyor located in the small seaside village of the same name just north of San Diego and within several miles of Stone's world headquarters in Escondido, California. The sauce contains fairly many ingredients, which normally may not be a good thing, but which I think, in this case, gives it a unique, complex flavor. Double Bastard Ale is second on the ingredient list, followed by vinegar, honey, lemon juice, sugar cane juice, garlic powder, habañero powder and several other things. The sauce is quite thin and features visible seeds and small chunks of chile floating in it. The bottle does not have a shaker top on it, so you have to be careful when you pour it lest you get way too much. Of course, the Double Bastard gargoyle is prominent on the label, which mimics that of the beer bottle.

So how does it taste? Well, taste is, of course, highly subjective. If I say, "It's fantastic," that doesn't mean anything. What tastes great to me may be inedible to someone else. My taste buds detect, right up front, the characteristic, distinctive, unmistakable habañero taste, combined with more sweetness than I expected. The taste of Double Bastard ale is not apparent; nor is there any bitterness associated with the hops in the beer (it's just neat to know it's in there). Double Bastard Double Burn Habañero Hot Sauce IS very hot, but the sweetness masks some of the chile heat. It's dangerously approachable, but it can sneak up on you because the heat of the habañeros is cumulative. A few dollops will add excellent flavor and spiciness to any dish that you would sprinkle Tabasco on, lending a more multi-dimensional taste sensation along with its heat.

Double Bastard Double Burn Habañero Hot Sauce now has a permanent place in my pantry, just as my dedicated beer refrigerator is never short of 22-ounce bottles of Stone Double Bastard ale. Talk about your perfect combinations...
top_profane.title: After One Taste, You'll Throw Stones at Tabasco Sauce

I've been vegan for about four years now. I've been stuck in a rut and considering going back vegetarianism after getting tired of eating the same vegan foods over and over again- especially breakfast. (How many tofu scrambles, overnight oats, and chia seed pudding can one person eat?) After reading such glorious reviews here on Amazon, I figured I'd try it.

These are entirely too complicated- far more so than a regular egg. If the product isn't cooked PERFECTLY, you're left with a soggy, goopy, expensive mess that ends up straight in the trash. One wrong move renders these eggs inedible, which sucks because this product is way more expensive than a carton of eggs. If you added any veggies or expensive spices like I did, then you'll end up pissy knowing all your expensive add-ins ended up in the trash with the rest.

I've gone through a carton and a half of this stuff, as well as various mushrooms, spinach, pink sea salt, and other ingredients trying to make a simple veggie fry to no avail. Not to mention that the taste and texture is horrendous- the powder reeks of rotten eggs when you mix it with water but none of that eggyness is retained in the final product. It also has a strange texture that is nothing like the texture of real eggs. I can't explain it, but basically this product isn't going to fool anyone into thinking this is real eggs. The only way I could stomach this was straight from the fridge, drowned in ketchup and with homemade smokey maple-tempeh.

One star. Does not replace the taste, texture, or convenience of actual eggs.

UPDATE: I made this update just to further emphasize how crap this product is. I decided to give it one more go by making vegan french toast this morning. Its french toast, right!l? It shouldn't be hard, right??? All you do is add the powder to ice-cold almond milk, whisk it together, dip in some bread, and fry over low heat- RIGHT???

Wrong!

The minute I added this product to almond milk, it balled up into these disgusting little balls of mush. No matter how much I whisked and beat it, it would not blend. I also tried to add rum, vanilla, and almond extact, so I'm not sure if that did it; either way, real eggs don't have that problem.

This is a crappy product for die-hard ethical vegans who want to convince themselves that whatever crap food theyre cooking isnt crap. As a vegan with her tastebuds still in tact who doesn't think that a bowl of quinoa with kale and tahini sauce is the most amazing dish in all of creation, I'm disappointed, and getting so tired of being vegan.
top_profane.title: Too finicky; doesn't taste like eggs at all

top_profane.text:
With your daily cup of coffee, you often want to have good quality and good taste at a fair price. But the canister i got was most vinegary, unnatural sour notes i had ever gotten despite having 3 more years of shelf life listed on it.

To clarify, i don't consider myself a snob but rather a value hunter, i love hunting good bargains for cups with great rich chocolatey notes, picking out the nuttiness and finding ways to improve even say a diner cup with milk and sugar to try to get the most out of my daily cups at a good price.

I've gotten great results with cheap but intensely flavorful coffees before, and even if a cup came out too bitter black adding sugar or milk often helped turn it into a dark chocolate coffee like concoction. I don't turn my nose up at price, only quality. And i've turned my nose up before at shitty burnt 10$ starbucks coffees for rich and chocolately and full bodied 3$ cafe bustelos before.

This coffee gave me high hopes after reading the reviews. Described as "excellent cup!" and "Great and nutty!". I recieved a canister that smelled alright, intense and a touch more acidic, and produced a extremely shitty cup with medium bitterness, and harsh unnatural vinegar and off lemon aftertones.

Yes. Vinegar and lemon aftertones.... in coffee. Not excessively bitter, no, not just bad or plain tasting. You heard that correct. It produced a extremely unnaturally sour cup with very little ingredient synergy like harsh vinegar and mild chocolate clashing, as well as harsh unnatural sourness like lemon water overmarking moderate coffee bitter undertones. The cups were absolutely unsalvagable after trying to brew 5-20 cups and checking equipment. I checked and washed, i brewed other cups of cheap but delicious nutty and chocolately coffees. They were excellent. I rebrewed this coffee many times, i tried drip brew, pourover, cold brew. Every result was the same. A uniquely shitty and irredeemable overpowering vinegar and lemon flavor that tasted like a cleaning product took over every cup. I had never heard of even the worst coffees producing a vile and vinegar like cup.

Only intensely bitter awful cups that could be mildly salvaged by cream and sugar. Putting cream and sugar in this made it worse. It made it this vile concotion that was like off lemon water with vinegar and coffee combined. From what i could narrow down, the coffee portion itself seemed like a mild bitterness and slightly nutty, very low or mild chocolate taste next to a freight train of bitter, awful mouth spitting vinegar and off lemon mouthfeel.

I'm not trying to be harsh, i really wanted to like this coffee, but i'm just trying to review the only reference i had. which is this canister that seems.. horribly off from everyone else's. [Perhaps it isn't the coffee, but perhaps a batch made after a machine was being cleaned? My canister tastes horribly like a lemon vinegar cleaning product and the coffee should be good for 3 more years and doesn't seem rotten, but there's a unmistakable really shitty and unnatural overpowering lemon and vinegar taste in mine, i don't know what the hell is going on.]

Anyways, i don't want to be too harsh, the price was okay for 5$, i don't consider myself a snob but after 5-10 attempts, not a single cup from the lemon vinegar canister i got seemed natural for a coffee. I don't think i got sick, but it was still vile to drink and i don't use that loosely. I'm happy to try coaxing a good chocolately or rich intense flavor from a value coffee, but after 5-20 cups of brew, not a single cup from the canister i got was very redeemable yet the notes don't seem marked anywhere.

I tried hard to like it, but it in all my life, was the only canister i ever had to be honest with myself and throw away. Mine just wasn't matching the other reviews notes of nuttiness or good flavor. I legitimately wonder if my cansister might have been a small batch after a cleaning operation, since others seemed to like theirs very much, while lemon and vinegar notes rarely if ever, happen even with the cheapest coffees. It tasted a lot like a harsh vinegar + lemon cleaning product. I was not trying to be harsh, but just fairly review the canister i got to be fair for other consumers.

[If Amazon or the merchant thought the canister might have been off, i would be happy to try giving a fair retrial. I did legitimately want to like this coffee, i just can't but help feel like either something was off with mine or it got cross mixed with a vinegar/lemon cleaning process during the roasting process ]
top_profane.title: I love a hidden gem, but my can wasn't it. [Unnatural Lemon/vinegary taste, Horrible results ]

top_profane.text: Ok, so everyone in my everyday life and my family and friends on Facebook should know by now that I am a devout PEPSI guy. I have never wavered. Coke is only drunk in an emergency, and that is when there is no water or other soda flavors around. If the restaurant doesn’t have Regular Pepsi, I go for Mr. Pib or Mello Yellow. But my life has been forever altered by a new soft drink in my life. For the first time I have started cheating on my love. It started off innocently enough, passing through the soda isle it caught my eye. The new Orange Vanilla Coke was staring back at me. I thought, humph, coke has really gone too far in the soda wars, who the hell do they think they are? Mixing the unappealing original flavor of Coke with Orange and Vanilla? It had to taste like hell reincarnate. But then my adventurous side took over and in full view of my beloved PEPSI I picked up the unassuming bottle of what was sure to be swill and purchased said bottle for 99 cents plus tax. I strolled out to the parking lot, cracked the bottle open, and took a drink. My friends what happened next was out of my control. A weird sensation over took me and it wasn’t the spike in my blood sugar levels......It was good. It was damn good. All of the sudden I felt a rush of shame come over me as I went in for a second, third, and fourth drink. This Orange Vanilla whore pulled me away from my true love, PEPSI, who had been with me through good times and bad, through the Friday nights of my high school years, watching my friends get shit faced while I sat on the couch and drank you. I let a little thing like Orange Vanilla flavoring come between a lifelong relationship, and I didn’t give a shit! For weeks Orange Vanilla Coke and I have been meeting at seedy gas stations and Walmart’s. I can’t stop myself, and the really sad part is I don’t want to. So be warned my friends, Orange Vanilla Coke will grip you, force you to drink her over ice, and before you know it, you will be willing to crawl over your own mother to get to her.
top_profane.title: Soda Whore

Oh Skittles!!! Thank you for creating a product that will live in our memories forever!! The laughter, vomit and foul words that your product caused was priceless.

Yesterday, My husband, myself and our 3 teenagers departed on a road trip. We pulled into a convenience store for snacks and a much needed potty break. It was there that my children found the skittles display housing bags of the new zombie skittles. My oldest just had to try them. I paid zero attention as I’m more of a health food nut but let them get whatever they wanted. We left the convenience store to continue on our journey.

The children, being children, tore into their bags to begin their sugar feeding frenzy as soon as we got in the car. They were all happily exchanging their loot and talking Of their dreams of Halloween (Or as they like to call it “spoopy month” (its a teen thing)). I remarking on how well they share with one another and how well they got along. We reached our destination and had a fabulous day out.

We got back in the car to return home around dusk. About 5 mins into our return journey, then fun began. (Please forgive any spelling or words after this point in the story. I’m either laughing hysterically or will be permanently banned from leaving reviews because of adult language). A loud, rumbling, screaming gag came out of my oldest child’s mouth. I jerked my head around from the passenger seat to make sure he wasn’t dying! I looked into his tearing eyes at which point he began to curse like a sailor. “WT?!!! These orange ones are absolutely bleeping disgusting!!! I’m not bleeping you!!! They taste like petrified human waste orifices (gotta keep it clean)!!!!! He screams while gagging on every other word.

He proceeds to go through the package and pull every orange skittle from the bag while continuing to curse and convince the rest of us that they really do taste like petrified orifices. I’m laughing because I don’t remember ever making petrified human orifices for dinner and I’m pretty sure he’s never gotten them at the school cafeterias (k. He might have). Then that sharing thing that I’ve been cultivating since they were born kicked in. His little sister thought it was funny so he offered one to her. She knows he’s a drama queen so she gladly accepts. She pops it in her mouth and chews. She smiles and looks at him with that preteen smirk and says, “They’re fiiiinne! Quit overreacting.”

Then oldest son offers it to me and to my middle son. Middle son is not adventurous nor does he truly trust either sibling so I eat mine first. “What’s the big deal? It taste like Bayer baby aspirin!” I said. The kids look at me like I’m speaking a foreign language. They have no clue about aspirin.

Middle son figures it’s safe. He pops it in his mouth, bites down and starts retching!! I look at him and I start to figure it out. But wait, we still have one more family member in the car who hasn’t played skittles roulette!!! Dad!!! Admittedly, I didn’t think this all the way through because my skittle tasted fine. If I knew then, what I know now, I would not have put the life of my children or myself in danger by risking my husband wrecking the car. I’m also going to digress a little here and inform you that half of the bag of skittles had been eaten before the first zombie skittle made its appearance.

“Here honey, the boys are crazy! Try it. I promise, it taste like baby aspirin. “

“Nope! Not gunna do it.” he says staring at the road.

“Seriously? Come on, it’s fine! They just don’t get baby aspirin. “ I plead.

He grabs one out of my hand. Puts it in his mouth. Bites down and... his face contorts. I mean literally turned itself inside out!! Everything outside his shirt turns dark pink in the fading sunlight. His mouth opens and there perched in the valley of the crevice of his tongue is a tiny, orange, nuclear atom with green smoke dancing from it. The smoke curls up in the roof of his mouth, doubles back and spirals over his teeth, escaping to his cheeks and into his watering eyes. He looks at me in horror and says, without moving his tongue, “IT’S ON ME!!!! ITS ON ME!!!” He closes his mouth, closes his streaming eyes and takes one huge gulp and swallows it! “The taste is still on my tongue!!!” He bellows!!! “Oh for bleeps sake!!! I ate bugs in the military and they didn’t taste this disgusting!!!”

By this time, I can’t breathe I’m laughing so hard. The kids are in the back seat convulsing in laughter. The car starts to deflate from lack of oxygen. The noxious green gas emitted by the tiny, orange, nuclear atom has turned my husband into what looked like a freed prisoner from Area 51. My middle son tells my oldest son to read the bleeping package. Oldest son reads the package but can’t find anything. Middle son snatches the package out of his hands and finds the warning label. “You’re a bleeping idiot!!! These are like beanboozled on acid!!!” These aren’t petrified human orifices!! These are dead baby zombie sphincter Flavored!!!”

I’m laughing even harder now. On the verge of passing out from lack of air. But, I’m feeling a little guilty about almost killing my husband so I take two of the 5 remaining and give one to my daughter and take one myself. Seriously?? You thought I was going to die alone?? Yeah, I’m not that good of a parent. I’m taking the little one it with me!!! I pop it in my mouth and pray. This is a lot for me because 1. I have an overactive gag reflex and 2. I’m not a person of faith. My daughter does the same. I look at her as she bites down. Nothing. Ok. My turn. I bite down and holy mother of donkeys!!!! The boys weren’t wrong in their description. My mouth flew open as the vomit elevated into my throat. I grasped at the armrest, unable to see, desperately searching for a way to get the window down as quickly as possible!!! My fingers burned as I made contact with the tiny, orange nuclear atom of death searing its way through my tongue. I tossed it as hard as I could through the open window. My head followed, mouth wide open, tongue hanging out, gulping in air in hopes of dissipating the green noxious gas. For the love of the planet!!! I have never tasted anything so putred in my life and if I do so again, I hope the lord will take me. Mortal words cannot adequately describe how bleeping awful those things are!!! I composed myself long enough to chuck the remaining skittles out the window. At which point my husband said that I was committing an act of animal cruelty.

With the orange culprits disposed of, my daughter went back to munching on the zombie skittles. Guess what?? It’s not just the orange ones!!! I hear the familiar death bellow and turn to see my daughter, the Area 51 alien, gagging. Her lovely brother is holding her down in the middle seat saying, “Nope!! You’re going to swallow it just like we did!!” Her stomach starts to growl and she starts to wretch. I scream through tears of laughter for them to let her go or be covered in vomit. I pop the lid off my cup and hand it to her. She vigorously scrapes her teeth on her tongue. Spitting venom into the cup.

The rest of the 2 hour trip home was spent creating new terms for the zombie skittles. We laughed. We cried. But best of all, we had fun. Thank you skittles for helping us create quality family time as well as new additions to the urban dictionary.

top_rant.text:
This is my last update, in which, unfortunately, I can’t recommend purchasing this 1-lb product…

You see, I proceeded to prevent the problem of the “evaporating divinity” by securing my freezer. I figured that hungry thieves were likely sneaking into the house to break off hunks of my “precious” while I slept at night. So, I put a heavy lock on the freezer door, then chained the entire frig closed, and finally placed a second big lock on the chain. No one except me could get at my delicious treasure! Hee, hee! And to make sure any unauthorized personnel were caught in the act (to be sternly reported to the police), I again got and set up a hidden “trail-cam.”

The next day I awoke expecting my Precious to be safe. Instead, I found a disaster! The chain was lying in SLAG-HEAPS at the foot of the refrigerator. It had been melted by intense heat! Its lock was CRUSHED. The other lock to the frig was CRACKED IN HALF! What had happened??? I grabbed the hidden trail-cam, saw it had been tripped by motion, and turned it on. There on the screen, I was amazed to see a FLASH OF WHITE LIGHT from which emerged the following creatures: little green elves, winged fairies, and giggling leprechauns. Together, they savaged my defenses, pried open the door, and swarmed onto my exposed tub of divinity. I expected them to abscond with their ill-gotten goods. But they did something totally bizarre. They each broke off a small piece, which they devoured simultaneously with happy sighs and grins. Any one of them could have taken the whole thing, but they seemed to have inhuman self-control. Then the white light flashed again and they were gone. And, with the fading of the screen, my trail-cam crumpled in upon itself and also vanished! Ah, they’d connived together to take my evidence with them…

Well. The “lesson” was obvious. The pure, concentrated goodness of the divinity was so intense that not only could it crack open space-time, it was MAGICAL. It could summon the creatures of imagination, empowering them to steal my jealously guarded property! Auuggghhhh!!!!

Angrily, I opened the freezer door, expecting just a shell of a container left. But surprisingly, it was intact. And there, inside, sitting primly in the middle, was a remaining 1-inch square of the pecan-embedded whiteness. Stunned, I took it into my hand, realizing that the magical creatures had left it there for me. I popped it into my mouth and savored it as it melted on my tongue, ashamed. They, who could have taken everything, SHARED their prize with me!

That’s when I realized that I could no longer buy the 1-lb product. I have no idea if you will experience the same problems as I: cracked-open space/time plus magical attractors. I may reside at a special nexus of the cosmos…or have somehow imbued my freezer with my own bizarre imagination. You may not have to worry about doppelgangers or fairies stealing your heavenly candy. But the product is too good to be hoarded. It must be shared, whether with family, friends, or visitors. And 1-lb is just not enough…

Yes, this deliciousness is too good to keep to oneself. It must be shared. And 1-lb is only sufficient for a brief taste by all before it “evaporates.” So, from now on, I will be purchasing the 3-lb “Large Divinity Gift Box (with Pecans)” (https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B076ZSKXDY), and recommending that others, likewise, do the same.

I’ve solved the problem of the evaporating divinity. The answer is simple. Get more!

-----------------------------------
2nd UPDATE: I can now report to you that I think I've solved the mystery of the evaporating divinity!

Suspecting foul deeds afoot, I set up a trail-camera to spy on my freezer at night. During the day, I only very rarely would take a very tiny little piece of my 1 lb hunk of deliciousness to pop upon my tongue as a treat, a reward, a meal-ender, and an itsy, bitsy teeny well-deserved desert! So how then did it the next morning seem to shrink by leaps and bounds?? Well, I suspected a thief! So, the trail-cam---which would only switch on when it sensed movement---would tell the story!

After setting it, I went to snuggle into my bed, sleeping soundly. The next morning, I jumped up very early to go look at the hidden trail-cam and was delighted to see it had been triggered! Viewing the footage, I was astounded after a FLASH OF BRILLIANT LIGHT to see ME there opening the frig, taking out the big hunk of delightfulness, and breaking off a BIG HUNK to pop into MY mouth! The light vanished and the door was closed with me gone. Was it... could it be... me SLEEP-WALKING in the night, unable to resist that hunk of glory sitting alluringly in the frig, getting myself a big midnight snack!??? Auuugghhhh.... I was greedily stealing from myself!?? Ah, what a humbling, apparent solution solving the mystery of the "evaporating" divinity...

But... I DON'T SLEEPWALK! Not only do I now not sleepwalk, I've never done so, ever! That fact means there's no easy solution to the mystery, after all. In fact, the only possible solution now is the amazing and utterly unavoidable reality that THE 1-LB CONCENTRATION OF PURE, UNDILUTED DIVINITY had somehow CRACKED OPEN the fabric of space-time, allowing another "me" in an ALTERNATE DIMENSION to reach across and snatch my marvelous treat! It's the only logical conclusion...

But, exhausted by the revelations, I staggered back to bed for a couple hours. When I arose, refreshed, I returned to the kitchen to find the trail-cam vanished and the 1-lb container now completely empty! Auuuggghhhh! The other "me" was playing with me, stealing my evidence plus all my remaining delicious goodness! THIS WILL NOT STAND!!! I will devise new, better safeguards for my "precious"!!! But to test my new security measures, I'll have to order yet another 1-lb container... no, not for gluttony or addiction! No, not at all! This is a necessary TEST of my enhanced security against dimensional, greedy wraiths!!!

I shall report back on the results...

------------------------------------
1st UPDATE: I don't like writing negative reviews. But in this case, I think I need to warn my fellow potential buyers. Something is deeply WRONG with this candy! Mysteriously, in the dead of the night, with no one else around, locked safe in my freezer... my first purchase evaporated! I thought it was just my imagination, so I ordered a second batch. But the same thing happened, even faster! Yikes! Yes, it's still the white divine nectar sprinkled with crunchy pecan flakes, incredibly smooth and delicious (hence my being forced to keep the 5 Star rating), but I purchased the BIG 1-POUND mound to last a long time providing me sweet treats, not disappear in my fridge! Each time I opened the freezer and look at it, it had shrunk drastically!

I'm the only person with access to it in the freezer, where it sits in its perfection waiting for me to break off a piece and pop it into my eager mouth. I know what you're thinking, but I swear it's not true! I'm sure the problem isn't ME! I'm certain that I only take a SMALL slice now and then... really, only a a few, small pieces... to top-off a meal or pump me up in the morning or top-off a hectic day. In fact, it's hardly even crumbs! In fact, I mostly just slide my finger along it to put a lingering taste onto my tongue. I admit I'd like to gobble it all down in one sitting, it's that good!!! But I have iron self-control. I do! So, I just use that whole mound of a full pound of goodness to give me very, very occasional, well-deserved, small---actually VERY tiny---little treats!

This is a mystery. As a science-fiction author, I suspect foul play. No, I'm not blaming the cooks for making it evaporate so I'll have to order replacements even more quickly! Likely the people producing this magnificent, pure delight don't even know what they've unleashed. Anyone making such exquisite deliciousness must also be pure, unselfish, and completely honorable. How could they be otherwise? But is their product so divine that it attracts ANGELS in the night, who steal my precious, yummy treasure??? Or... is it worse than that, prowling DEMONS torturing me with its excessive shrinking, making it increasingly vanish from my sight???

I must solve this dastardly crime! I will not have my wonderful prize increasingly stolen from me in the night! The only course of action is to ORDER A THIRD BATCH, this time with new safeguards. I have an obligation to do this, not at all an obsessive yearning! Yes, that's a plan. I'll report back on what I find, so that you too might not be frustrated by horrific, evaporating divinity! In this evil, chaotic world there's so little that's pure and good, giving us a brief moment of relief and joy, it must be protected!

How can something that feels so right be so wrong? It's a cosmic puzzle that I must solve...
-----------------------------------------

ORIGINAL REVIEW: This is the real, pure deal...

This is the real divinity deal: 1) pure, simple, minimal ingredients; 2) big hunk easy to store in freezer and break off small pieces; and 3) melt-in-your mouth delightful hit. I researched the different divinity products and this was by far the best of the lot on paper, then "ground-truthed" on delivery and tasting. In a world where fat, cholesterol, and saturated fat rule mass-packaged products (all bad for us older folks who still want an occasional taste of heaven), this is a relatively healthy delight.

top_rant.text:
I got this [[ASIN:B07QBQ8Q33 Base Culture Keto Bread]] from Whole Foods through Prime Now, because I was running low on the [[ASIN:B07GJXG1VH Sola Bread]] that I usually ordered from Meijer through Instacart grocery home delivery, since I am unable to drive (thanks vaccine injuries/Big pHARMa!🤬). However, as far as I know, Meijer is the only local store that sells [[ASIN:B07GJXG1VH Sola Bread]] and because at the time that I ordered THIS [[ASIN:B07QBQ8Q33 Base Culture Keto Bread]] I didn’t have anything other than the bread to order from Meijer, and I had several things that I needed to get from Whole Foods and I didn’t want to have to order enough stuff at TWO places to meet the $35 minimum at each place for free delivery (meaning at least $70 total, not counting tips) .

Not to mention that I’ve been hesitant to keep using [[ASIN:B07GJXG1VH Sola Bread]] because though I’ve asked the manufacturer at least 2 or 3 times, whether this bread contains GMO (Genetically Modified Organisms) ingredients, I’ve never gotten a response. Because I am anti-GMO as much as possible, not knowing whether the [[ASIN:B07GJXG1VH Sola Bread]] was GMO or not was very concerning. Nevertheless, reluctantly, I kept getting the [[ASIN:B07GJXG1VH Sola Bread]] because my mom really liked/likes it and it seemed like there was NO other keto bread that was good, BOTH in terms of flavor AND texture. I have tried SEVERAL different keto breads over the years, and pretty much ALL of them had one thing or another wrong with it. Whether it was the keto bread by Julian Bakery that was ABSOLUTELY INEDIBLE (horrible taste & godawful texture, and even putting it in the toaster didn’t change it one iota!), or the keto breads by the Great Low Carb Baking Company which had a better texture but an “off” taste, or completely homemade breads using alternative flours like one of the [[ASIN:1609611543 Wheat Belly]] book recipes for bread using chickpea flour, which were just outright disgusting, or whether they’re potentially GMO, or a combination of those factors or whatever else, they were ALL a HUGE disappointment!

Until I came across this [[ASIN:B07QBQ8Q33 Base Culture Keto Bread]] at Whole Foods PrimeNow, I never knew that there was a better option out there. Because aside from [[ASIN:B07GJXG1VH Sola Bread]] the ONLY other keto bread that came remotely close to being decent substitute for bread, was the homemade breads using [[ASIN:B0096E57Z4 LC Foods]] (www dot holdthecarbs dot com) mixes flours. Yet I was also unable to find out if the [[ASIN:B0096E57Z4 LC Foods]] flours was GMO or not, and on top of that, though the texture of the bread was practically perfect (from what I remember, anyway — its been awhile since I’ve had that bread!) there was definitely something “off” about the flavor, like some kind of weird taste/aftertaste.

Which is why I am SO glad to have found this [[ASIN:B07QBQ8Q33 Base Culture Keto Bread]]!

  Because in my honest opinion, BOTH the flavor AND the texture of this keto bread is pretty much perfect and about as close to REAL bread as one can get, without all the carbs of REAL bread. Best of all, it has NO ingredients that are GMO, and there is NO unpronounceable & undecipherable chemical crap in it, NOTHING that you’d need a master’s degree or Ph.D in chemistry to figure out what it is that you’re consuming. Nothing other than just almond butter, almond flour, ground flaxseed, arrowroot flour and other regular ingredients (like eggs, salt, water, etc.) — and yet, this bread tastes wonderful, toasts up nicely, and has a nice texture & crumb.

Although I wish the [[ASIN:B07QBQ8Q33 Base Culture Keto Bread]], was less than 4 net carbs per slice, its still nice to be able to have a slice or two of buttered toast with my morning coffee or maybe a sandwich at lunch or a midnight snack of keto hot chocolate and toast with nut butter. In addition, I wish that “Base Culture” offered mixes for these breads so that people could make these breads in the form/shape/type that they want and to be able to add other ingredients and/or toppings to. For example, I ❤️ this bread, but I would really like to have it in the form of hot dog buns, so that I can use it with hot dogs, sausages/bratwurst, and stuff like that, instead of having to use bread which gives a different feeling. Moreover, I really like sesame seeds and poppy seeds, and if “Base Culture” offered this bread as a mix, for people to make and bake on their own, I would DEFINITELY buy it, and use it to make buns or bread with sesame seeds and/or poppy seeds. I have a hot dog bun pan I got from LC Foods, that has been sitting unused for ages, because I’ve been unable to find a good recipe or mix for keto hot dog buns — and I think that THIS bread would make GREAT hot dog buns, if it was available in a mix.

Oh and for those complaining that the carb count is wrong, and that this bread is not keto……………….maybe you got some non-keto loaf (like the paleo loaf, which is different) by mistake. Because I got the keto loaf a few days ago, and as you can see in the picture, it is definitely just 4 net carbs per slice, which IMHO is on the tad higher side for keto bread, I think the taste & texture of the bread is so wonderful that the carb count is excusable. That being said, I DO wish this bread was a bit chewier rather than softer, like it is. But since the chewiness factor comes from wheat gluten, I guess they can NOT make it chewier and still be gluten free. Nevertheless, I wish that they could find/use some alternative, like xanthan gum. Last but not least, given that this bread is in/around the $7 range for a loaf, I wish that it was even just a little bit bigger, and that there was more and/or larger slices. THAT (the price and the quantity/value for the money) is one of a couple/few reasons I’m giving this bread 4 stars instead of 5. I mean I get that ingredients like almond flour and almond butter are expensive, but still $7 or so per loaf is still pretty pricey, and even a dollar or two less, especially when buying it in bulk, would go a long way to improving my opinion and would raise the rating to 5 stars.

Regardless, I’m still going to continue to get [[ASIN:B07QBQ8Q33 Base Culture Keto Bread]], and in fact, the next time I order from Whole Foods, I’m planning to really stock up on it like I did with the [[ASIN:B07GJXG1VH Sola Bread]] and buy at least a half dozen loaves or so, to stick in the deep freezer/chest freezer in the garage. That way, in case the bread goes out of stock temporarily or goodness forbid, is discontinued (please, PLEASE Powers-That-Be, don’t ever let that happen!), I’ll hopefully still have at least a few or several loaves in the freezer to tide me over until a decent substitute can be found. Because, all in all, [[ASIN:B07QBQ8Q33 Base Culture Keto Bread]] is probably hands-down the BEST keto bread that I’ve ever had so far, and in my opinion, its probably the closest that it can get to regular bread and still be keto. Other low-carb/keto bread companies really need to take lessons from “Base Culture” in how to create DELICIOUS low-carb/keto bread WITHOUT toxic GMOs or chemical crap.  

I ❤️ this [[ASIN:B07QBQ8Q33 Base Culture Keto Bread]]!!

★☆★☆ 4 STARS!!!!!!!!!!!!

Man oh man, where do I start? Um... so a crappy gut microbiome is truly awful and you can terrify yourself and read up on all manner of scary factoids about it. One major downside is coming down with a CDiff infection which is precisely what I did recently when I knew pretty much nothing about gut health. All I kept seeing was that 20% of people who succumb to it are destined to suffer over and over and OVER and just can't seem to get rid of it. Even the lucky 80% are often stuck coming down with it again within days to weeks of completing the scary heavy duty antibiotics needed to kill it, and having to go through another round. I won't go into the details, just know that it's awful.

So while I was on the medication and scaring myself with Dr. Internet, I came across several articles and personal accounts talking about drinking Kefir inbetween antibiotic pills (mine was 1 pill every six hours, so I drank it 3 hours after each pill, thus leaving another 3 hours before the next one), and then especially drinking it once the antibiotics were done. I was buying a store-bought plain version and downing a quart per day (no, I'm not a doctor and I'm not telling anyone to do the same thing, just saying what I personally did) - 8 oz every 6 hours. Once I was done with the antibiotics, I was still consuming a quart daily - 8 oz 4 times a day.

I'm not really a fan of yogurt at all, and the first cup of store bought stuff I drank I was making ridiculous gaggy faces and saying "Blech!" but a funny thing happened. I think my body instantly recognized that I needed the good stuff in kefir because even though I never liked the taste, I DREAMED about drinking my next cup. Even right after I drank one down, I was like "Man, I can't wait to have the next one!" and THAT is saying something because you have absolutely zero appetite when you have CDiff. I was even gagging on plain bone broth or water.

So then I started gagging on the price tag of $3-4 a bottle which is a quart size. That was $3-4 a day! I looked up making my own Kefir and was surprised to read that a lot of store brands use thickeners and such (like cellulose or pectin), PLUS apparently they need to pasteurize the product for shelf life. I was like "Well how much good bacteria survives pasteurizing??"
A lot of browsing later led me to here, and I ended up purchasing a Tbsp of grains. I had never made kefir before, but the paper that came with the grains had a link to the e-book and that was very informative in getting started! It took a few days for my grains to wake up and make thick creamy stuff, so I tossed the first few batches until I woke up one morning to something thicker where I could see slight separation at the top and bottom.
We keep our AC set to 68 most of the time here and I did not need to warm up my kefir bottles sitting on my granite counter in the kitchen. I have a digital temperature gun and the bottles almost always read around 71.5 degrees F. In fact, these little guys are so active and robust that I wake up and strain the kefir at 8am, and fill the new mason quart jars with the strained grains (1 tbsp in each jar) and fresh milk (I fill the milk up to right where the quart bell jar begins to curve in toward the cap) and then put them in the fridge until I take them out and put them on the counter at 1pm. I do this every day, and it results in the perfect creamy kefir at 8am for me every morning. The grains multiplied like crazy and I've thrown out some at least 3 times and it's only been slightly over 3 weeks. When I strain the kefir in the morning, I get 3 regular mason jars filled to the brim with kefir. The key to getting it nice and creamy is pouring the quart fermenting jar contents into a bowl through a nylon strainer (bought a nice cheap one on amazon) and then making sure you use your utensil (I just use a plastic spoon) to scrape that amazing cream off of the bottom of the strainer into the bowl. Tap the strainer a few times onto the top of the bowl, scrape again. A lot of times I'll use my plastic spoon to sort of spread the grains around in the strainer and you'll actually see them get cleaner and more defined as the cream goes through the strainer and the grains stay behind. Then I do a final cream scrape off the bottom of the strainer into the bowl, and scoop and newly cleaned grains into 2 new (meaning freshly washed) mason quart jars and fill with milk. I cover them each with a coffee filter (not the cone kind, a regular old fashioned one. They're super cheap), and then screw the metal ring lid on (I threw out the metal flat disk part of the lids) over the filter. It ends up looking like some kind of copacabana drink container. I give the bowl of strained kefir a stir and then pour it (my bowl has a slight spout) into little clean mason jars and cap them and put them in the fridge. They are for drinking the next day after a 24 hour fridge second ferment. You can drink it right away but they just get SO creamy and wonderful the next day! So every day I put the new ones in the fridge behind the 3 I made yesterday which are ready for me to drink today. I hope that makes sense the way I've written it!

I would compare the flavor of the fresh kefir vs store bought as "punchier". It's a bit tangier and... well just... punchier. I tried putting zest or fruit in the strained kefir as it did it's second ferment but I really needed to get it into something that my family would also drink. That's when I read about making smoothies with it. Oh my gosh how did I NOT KNOW about this!!?? It's SO GOOD! You don't taste any tang or punch with fruit and flavor blended in especially if you're adding frozen stuff to make it super cold or frosty.

Here are our favorites:
8 oz kefir from the fridge
1 frozen banana (bananas are a prebiotic!)
Dash of cinnamon (or pumpkin pie spice)
Splash of honey to your liking. Also you can use real maple syrup instead to change it up.
Blend and enjoy.
(Note: I also add a handful of frozen kale or spinach, it doesn't affect the taste and adds nutrition).
Alternatively you can add a spoonful of almonds (almonds are a prebiotic too!) if you have a beefy blender. We use a Vitamix.

Favorite of my kid's and mine:
16 oz Kefir
1 room temp banana
1 1/2 cup frozen fruit (we buy this frozen brand that has mango, pineapple, coconut and dragon fruit)
Handful of frozen kale or spinach
Honey to taste
Blend.
We've made this for several friends now and everyone has loved it!

My personal favorite:
8 oz Kefir
1 Frozen Banana
Cut a tangerine in half and squeeze the juice into the blender
Handful of Frozen spinach or kale
Honey to taste
You can also add vanilla to give it an orange julius feel
Blend

I'm sure you can see where I'm going with all of this. Use your imagination. We use banana with all of them because it's a fantastic prebiotic food. I've been experimenting with some of the other "top prebiotic foods" like oatmeal and apples but I wasn't a fan of that combo. Flavor was fine but the consistency was a little wonky. I'll play around with it. Throwing cocoa and almonds in was pretty darn good with a frozen banana!

I have read several places that fresh kefir is a lot more potent than store bought (for the reasons I mentioned about 9 pages ago... sorry I'm writing so much!) so I drink 16 -20 oz a day now instead of 32. Basically a banana/kale/tangerine shake for breakfast (this stuff makes a great meal replacement that actually makes me full and satisfied for hours) and then the banana/frozen tropical fruit/spinach one after dinner as a dessert with my kid. My husband likes the frozen banana&cinnamon one. They are both drinking kefir every day for nearly 3 weeks. It was a small amount at first as my kefir grains grew but within a week and a half we were all up to a full cup of kefir per smoothie. (And I have 2 because I'm the one that clearly had an F Minus in the gut department. I have some catching up to do!)

Seriously I have to throw away grains every couple of days! I wasn't doing that at first and the kefir was separating too fast. As long as I keep it to one good sized tbsp per quart jar of milk it comes out perfectly. Like I said, I strain at 8am, fill fresh jars with milk and grains and then put them in the fridge. I take them out at 1pm and let them sit until 8am the next day. Doesn't mean that'll be your schedule - it could be totally different at your house with your milk, but just know that I've been doing this and the couple hours in the fridge doesn't bother them at all in mine. So if you're struggling to get a daily schedule without waking up to total whey/curd separation (happened to me once!) you can totally try the fridge vaycay trick! If I know i'm sleeping in the next day or unable to strain first thing in the morning then I just leave them in the fridge longer and take them out at, say, 3 or 4pm instead of 1pm. I'm just saying the timing is flexible and it's so easy to do this!

I haven't felt this great in years and once I got the hang of what I was doing, making the kefir is super easy. Takes me a few minutes in the morning to strain it and start fresh batches and that's really it. I wash everything in the dishwasher (although I did invest in a cheap bottle brush to get the inside of the mason jars of any hardy residue)

top_rant.text:
Amour Tomato Paste, Double Concentrated 4.5 oz. tube is my purchase. I LOVE THIS AMOUR TOMATO PASTE❣️ It has the best feedback record on Amazon! A lot of praise for this product, from home cooks & chefs.
It’s made by Italian Chefs in 🇮🇹 Italy! Non GMO, VEGAN, BPA FREE! It arrived in Hawai’i in an envelope in tact!

I opened the tomato paste tube & tasted it! I was very pleasantly surprised at the fresh tomato taste! It was really good! Not bitter, not sweet, no salty, no metallic taste from being packaged in a metal tube. Which I had concerns about! And most importantly it’s fresh not stale! With a two year shelf life and a expiration date of 2021! Big points in that department for me, I wasn’t sure how long it would keep. Or if I’d use it all before it goes bad! It says to refrigerate after opening. No problem there, I had planned on doing that already!

In fact I’d go as far as to say, “it was better than any tomato paste, I’ve ever had out of a tin can! We all know that almost nobody ever uses, a whole can of tomato paste at once!
Unless their cooking in bulk.

Most recipes only call for a couple of tablespoons per dish.
It just so happened I was cooking Mexican food when the package arrived. So I put it to the test, I added 2 Tbsp. To my ground turkey meat filling for my taco’s & burritos. It was perfection! It nicely coated the meat, and gave it a tomato boost. I usually can only acquire by adding a can of tomato sauce & a couple of tablespoons of tomato paste!

I also added some to my Spanish rice, I was making from scratch! This stuff is the bomb! No wonder they named it AMOUR❣️You fall in love with the taste! 😍 My family noticed the freshness of the tomato taste too! They even ask what I did differently, GUILTY AS CHARGED! I’m always looking to up my game in cooking! I have tons of cookbooks, and I’m addicted to the food network!

I read a lot of feedbacks before I purchase anything, especially food & spices. Is it just me, or is Amazon like having a support group for foodies? 👩🏼‍🍳🥣💞

FOR THE SELLERS: AMOUR COMPANY
MAHALO NUI ALOHA YOUR TOMATO PASTE IS “ONO” That means good in 🌺 Hawaiian❣️ When we like the taste of something really special, we say it’s “ONOLICIOUS” a play on words used in Hawaii we speak pigeon English, it’s slang for the word delicious. GOOD KINE GRINDS! I think you can translate that? It means very good kind of grub!

Pigeon English 101:
Time to broke da mouth, that means it time for good eat’s now! 🌶🌽🥬🍅🌮🌯🥙🍣🍱🥟🍤🥠🥢🍛🍝🍜🍲🥗🥘🥔🥕🍠🍗🥖🍧🥭🍍🥥🍓🍒🍌🍉🥝
Time to broke da elbow, that means it’s time to throw back some liquor! It’s 5:00 everywhere in Hawai’i it’s always happy hour!
🍹🍻🥂🍷🥃🍶

My first thought was, after tasting this! I need to re-order more AMOUR TOMATO PASTE ASAP!

I went back to order this after reading feedbacks, while I was online the combo tomato paste & Anchovy Paste totally sold out! I had to wait to order it! Buyers are hip to this product, they pounce when they offer deals at discount prices! Be aware!

There was a buy three option, but I wanted to order one & and try it before ordering in bulk. It past the test with flying colors! I would highly recommend this product!

This Amour company offers variety pack’s that fly off the shelf. Great prices & the convenient’s of having it on hand, in the refrigerator and in the pantry. I ordered the tomato paste double concentrated, more bang for your buck I was thinking!

I ordered the Anchovy Paste too, it’s a great flavor boost for roasting vegetables like cauliflower, heirloom carrots, and turnips etc.
Just perfect to keep on hand when a recipe calls for anchovy’s.

Amour Brand also sell several tomato flavors, sun dried tomato, pesto’s and garlic too! I’m going to order the tomato with basil next time! This is definitely going to be a staple in my home from now on!

Shipping Tip:
First I need to say, I’m a Amazon Prime customer. It is so worth it, to me in particular to be a prime customer. Because I live in Honolulu, Hawai’i. Island Of Oahu

I have had to master shipping things! To protect all my purchase from being damaged, in route to the Islands. I ship them separately!

Especially the food items, I know the boxes get bounced around. It’s just smarter, to wait until I see a confirmation that my other orders have been shipped already and are in route that I can track! Sometimes that means I have to wait two to three days to order.

Before I order more items, that I know are fragile, and hold a high risk of damages. I wait an order them separately! So far I’ve been very successful in my strategy.

I order my toiletries separately, like my hair care products. I ship all of them together in the same box. I Never mix anything together with my food items.

All orders of food that’s heavy, like can goods or glass jars, or perishables. That I buy in bulk or by the case. I ship separately!

Sometimes Prime doesn’t cover free shipping on heavy items. And I’m ok with that, if it’s the only way I can acquire them. I feel thankful that some sellers offer the items, with an additional shipping cost!

I have them all shipped individually to Hawai’i. The only time I order many items together, is if they are the same kind of items in weight & packaging. Like freeze dried fruits, great for adding to cereal & jello! They taste as fresh as fresh frozen fruit & vegetables! My pantry is totally stocked to the gills. In case of bad tropical storms or God forbid a 🌊 Hurricane. We don’t get Tornadoes 🌪 in Hawai’i On the plus side Freeze Dried Containers, doesn’t stand to get broken or smashed if shipped together!

Better safe then sorry! Nobody wants to loose sleep, over something that can be shipped within a few days apart. I Feel blessed to even find them online! If their not sold on island in Hawai’i.

Good luck shop safe & shop smart online! Think before you order ten things from food to decoderant, and expect it to arrive in perfect condition. After it’s been bounced around on a truck or airplane. Have you watch the news, when they show delivery people bouncing tv’s over a fence. Because their so tired over worked. Or just bit off more than they could chew, taking a delivery job! Delivery people are only human, try to think about making their job a little easier. Try to order things ahead of time, not at the last minute. And take into consideration somebody has to lift these heavy boxes. Then maybe just maybe, your packages will arrive nicely. Everything in its proper place. Be kind to one another out there! Just put yourself in the shoes and mindset of others. Think before you snap at somebody, we’re all just doing our time on planet earth! Trying to earn a buck, so we can get home to our loved ones❣️ Peace Luv & Aloha from the third rock from the Volcano🌋🗿🏝🐚🌈🌺🏄🏼‍♀️🐾👣🐋🐠🐡🐙🦀🐬🦎🦋🐸🦚🦢🦜🕊🌴🎋🌞👙👡👒🕶💅🏻💋👩🏼
