top_rant.text:
Please let me preface this review with two pieces of information. First, I have never dieted before. Not one bit. I always thought of it the way Garfield explained it: Diet is "Die" with a "T". Second, I have been drinking these shakes for 5 days. Basically, I have no experience to speak of with dieting and I haven't been drinking these shakes for long at all.

TLDR? At 38 I have been chronically fatigued and in such constant pain I had lost much hope of feeling better ever, and often feared the very thought of getting any older. I have close older friends and family who are more sturdy and have more vigor than I do! After 5 days of drinking these shakes I feel so fantastic that I had to write this huge long thesis on why I am so grateful for this product and am excited to see how I feel in a week, a month, a year from now! Thank you, Ketolent!!

Want to make your eyes bleed? You have been warned! Here is the rest of my review:

Here is my ridiculously long backstory and why I am giving this product 5 stars after only 5 days of use...

The backstory:

I am sedentary. I work an office desk job. I test software. I get into and sometimes don't move for hours as I get into a task. I also work overtime - even if typically just a bit each day - all the time. I have an insane commute of 75 miles a day in sometimes heavy city traffic, which typically logs me in at another 3+ hours of sitting, give (sometimes a lot if there are accidents, etc.) or take (not usually much, lol!). When I get home - exhausted from sitting and thinking and then bookended by concentrating on avoiding insane drivers for 12+ hours - I have had zero energy. With this latest job and the crazy commute, I haven't often had energy during the week to play the dear-to-my-heart video games. Yep. So burnt out and exhausted I haven't been able to game with my laptop in my bed desk! Netflix night - every night. And that was about all I could handle. Cooking? Sometimes I would have to get a snack to keep my blood sugar up so that I could stand and cook or I would beg my boyfriend to make me food. The thought of doing YOGA exhausted me.

My weight - though down from when I experienced a near system failure while I was working graveyard shift a couple years ago - is high at 164lbs and 5'5". My energy is low - abysmally low. My brain is foggy. To think at work and concentrate on my commute I subsist on caffeine and sugar to get me by - so I can think and not completely crash and burn. I've had increasing injuries in my back and joints. I've felt like I have arthritis. The pain I feel is often intense. Though it makes me feel weak and incapable of functioning normally when I cannot do things people older than me can do easily, I know that the level of pain I have plowed through to do what needs doing - work and house chores and errands - is legitimate and serious and certainly not all in my head, nor me being a wimp.

The sad truth is that I am exhausted. Every day has been filled with endless fatigue and brain fog. I have horrible blood sugar crashes way too frequently. I hurt all over, all the time. Intense, deep pain in my back and joints. I have tried to exercise but it leaves me feeling worse and sore deep in my bones even with minor impact activities like walking and yoga. I get terrible migraines that last hours or days. I don't eat much at any given time as I don't like feeling overfull, but I am quickly hungry over and over again all day long and have sugar crashes when I don't eat every few hours. I have terrible menstrual periods with intense pain that can make me bedridden for a whole day.

I feel so miserable - in pain, physically and mentally exhausted that even though I know all of these terrible symptoms are real and intense... I don't know how to approach it with a doctor anymore. I feel like it will sound preposterous like I am a hypochondriac. In the recent past, I have gotten various typical exercise recommendations and/or meds to control pain. I haven't gone there but I know if I asked about nutrition I would likely get the typical low-fat, low cal diet recommendations and I cannot imagine feeding my body less when I barely have the energy to get by on what I eat now! None of it has worked and I hate to take serious pain pills. I take OTC pills enough as it is! It sometimes gives me digestive issues and I have to take antacids... I guess you get it. I am a wreck. I have been for years. At 38 it is only getting worse and worse and in my heart I feel very young but my body is old, old, old.

Looking to make a real change:

So, Keto. I may have never dieted but I've looked. I have tons of health, diet, and cookbooks, though I never diet and rarely cook much of anything fancy. I guess I kept looking for something I could do. Something that sounded like it could work, that I could do. Something that would break the bank. Or force me to spend endless hours each week cooking when I don't have much free time as it is. The closest I came to starting something was a vegan smoothie diet, but it seemed expensive. I didn't have a great blender. What if it didn't work out and I spent $$$ on a Vitamix. Or I got a cheaper one and my diet failed cause my blender sucked? Also, no one in my house is going to go vegan, or vegetarian, with me. Meatless Mondays wouldn't even work here. I would be on my own. You see, I clearly think too much, but I also felt like I knew what wouldn't work for me. Why start to fail? I could maybe do any number of diets if I wanted to but... people fail at them all the time or fall off the wagon or rebound. I didn't want that to be me. But Keto - I could look at all those meals and though in my heart I knew I would long for bread and pasta and rice and sugar... It was all food I could see my family eating, even if they had theirs on a bun or a bed of rice. This could work, but... cooking... I still don't have time to cook... so I poked around... I stopped eating mini-donuts every day at work. I poked around more...

Finding Ketolent, and sweet relief:

And I found Ketolent. It made me think of the Soylent a friend had sworn worked wonders for them, but it was Keto. It seemed a bit pricey... a minimum of $90 for a month of powder? Yikes. I've had shakes in the past - not to diet but to have a swift meal - and even fancy, expensive brands from Sprouts are half the cost of this stuff or less! I hemmed and hawed. I put it in my cart. I thought about this and that. I figured it was $3 a day? That really is nothing... why was I on the fence? I just was, but then I caved. I got the vanilla kit for $105, so I would have a bottle for work and the oil all ready to go and give it a whirl. Why not? What do I have to lose? $105? Sure! Woo! Let's do this. Let's tell my boyfriend I am doing this and feel a little crazy but what is $105 for a month of smoothies?

Gosh, I hope it tastes okay...

It arrives swiftly in the mail despite not being Prime 2-day shipping. I let it sit there for a week. I think about it. (I am ridiculous, I know!!) On Friday, when I decide to work from home because I was having severe pain in my shoulder from who knows what. I sat against an ice pack all day and drank a smoothie for lunch. It tasted great! I wasn't hungry for hours... "This isn't too shabby!", I thought to myself. And I had one the next day, and the next, for lunch. They still tasted great, had a great creamy consistency and they were keeping me from feeling so snacky. Over the weekend I tried to eat "better" - low sugar / lower carbs - but wasn't beating myself up over anything. Then on Monday, I had a shake in the morning. I felt really good. Like, really good. I didn't need a coffee to scrape myself together and begin work after my commute. I felt clear headed and... just damned good. I drank a Monster cause I felt weird not drinking one but I didn't need it, really. The satiety lasted almost all the way to lunch which I took 7 hours after my breakfast. I had a Monster energy drink in the late afternoon. I drove home and I didn't need to eat immediately after getting home. My boyfriend and I took a walk! I felt good! I made an egg + veggie dish cooked to keto-friendly specifications and a fatty drink when I was a little peaked at bedtime - when I usually get famished and have to snack on carbs. The next day - today - I did the same having a shake for breakfast. This time I didn't have a Monster in the morning. I felt GREAT! I felt more focused, clear and alert than I felt with more sleep and more food and more caffeine. I had a shake for lunch... had some stressful issues with something I was testing that made me start to feel a bit of a headache so since I am prone to migraines I drank a Monster and took some Excedrine as I didn't want to deal with that on top of my work. But after the stress was over I felt great again. I came home and walked again with my boyfriend, then made an omelet with veggies and bacon. I sit down to write this and realize I have felt better in the last two days than I have felt so many years ago as a kid, a teen and a young person. I feel like a different person. I feel clear headed, a bit energetic - for me! - and I realized... I hadn't had joint pain for the last day.

If you have made it through this whole backstory... I am sorry! And thank you! And, I hope this helps you if you have experienced similar symptoms. If you have then while I cannot give you medical advice, I can still suggest you consider trying this product. It is $3 a meal. It can make your life easier with less to buy, plan, cook, everything. All you do is blend a couple shakes at the beginning of your day (I use a cheap old Magic Bullet) and go. I like them cold. I recommend that though the taste and texture are such that I cannot imagine I would despise them at room temperature either. Then at night have a good easy dinner.

Bottom line... I hadn't heard of Ketolent until a week or two ago. I have been drinking these shakes for only 5 days but I after having been at such a rock bottom for so long I am flabbergasted that within 5 days I have transformed into feeling actually good for the first time since I cannot remember when. I am just so intensely grateful for these past two days of BETTER that all I can do is write this horridly long-winded review to try to help anyone else out there like me, and to be thankful for the researchers who began the Keto diet craze and to be endlessly thankful to the makers of Ketolent for the best two days of my life in years. THANK YOU, THANK YOU!

So, I am finally at the end but I am a chronic review writer and review updater. I am new to the "dieting" thing - no, let's call it a healthier thing! - but I still plan to update you with my progress for a while to see more about the long-term effects of consuming Ketolent for 2 meals a day, but to start: Today, 04/17/2018, I am feeling pretty darned positive at day 5 of consuming Ketolent shakes. I am not ultra interested in weight-loss - I am more concerned about feeling better - but I know that at 5'6"/165lbs I need to lose weight. So, I got a scale (never had one of my own before, ha!) will take some measurements for this current me and then I will be back to update this review in at most a month - perhaps sooner if I have something amazing to share. Until then I truly hope this can help even one other person out there, and I hope that no matter what route you take that you begin to feel better than you do now, too! Good luck to you, friends!

UPDATE 04/25/2018: So it's been a couple weeks now. I love these things. I am having 2 a day + a nice dinner and fatty, healthy snacks and it's wonderful! I'm in ketosis and I have more energy than I can recall having in any recent history. I go walking most evenings with my boyfriend now as I am not coming home exhausted or in a blood sugar crash like I used to so often be at 7-8pm. Also, weight is peeling off. I am not bloated, not gassy and I seem to be down from 165 to 160lbs - all without feeling like I am dieting. All while feeling fantastic! Going to get my boyfriend and maybe a coworker to try this stuff out. I am so pleased. Just wonderful stuff and highly recommend this product to help get you on track if you are starting Keto and/or perhaps if like me you have little time to cook 3 square meals a day!
top_rant.title: In 5 days I feel better than I have felt in 10+ years!

top_rant.text:
I'm giving this product 4 stars instead of 5 because I need to get used to the 'taste' and how it slightly changes the texture of the drink (tea or hot cocoa) I put it in...
I would give it 5 stars for performance because I was relieved of hip and 'seat' pain within minutes of drinking my first serving!!
I am pregnant (just around 13-14 weeks) with my 3rd. My first 2 pregnancies were very close and my back muscles (which weren't very strong to begin with) took a serious beating with carrying baby #2 while taking care of baby #1 (having to carry him around until he could walk, carrying him asleep in his car seat, working and going to school, way too busy, now that I look back on how I lived during that 2nd pregnancy). After baby #2 was born, it took me a very very very long time to heal. And even when she turned 3, I would occasionally feel pain in my 'seat' and I just knew it was my body still mending from the ordeal of close pregnancies. To make matters 'worse', I have an umbilical hernia that I didn't know was what I had, and my stomach muscles never completely closed after baby #2 was born. Fast forward 6 years later and I am unexpectedly expecting, and by the end of my 10th week, I could already feel strains on my back and my hips. Normal stuff under normal pregnancy circumstances, but I was dreading the days ahead, knowing how weak my muscles are and how vulnerable my belly muscles also are with the hernia...
I had never heard of gelatin (crazy, I know) in a 'supplement' form. I was having a very rough first trimester when a very good friend of mine who's extremely dedicated to healthy eating and lifestyle emailed me and said she had been looking into gelatin, and thought of me, and then she shared this link through Amazon. I proceeded to read about gelatin, then checked the reviews on here, and decided last week I had nothing to lose: my hips were bothering me, I walked with a limp, and my back was already killing me too. My 'bump' is already big (people expect me to say I'm 20 weeks along, not just 12), and I think it's because of the hernia that had prevented my stomach muscles to go back together completely. I couldn't wait for the gelatin to arrive yesterday and as soon as UPS rang my bell, I tore open the box, and tried what Catuk said in her review, to mix properly. Unfortunately, that didn't work for me as it became grainy and clumpy, and when I put my hot tea in, it clumped and looked really gross. I am already having a hard time with foods and smells because of the pregnancy, and looking at what was in my cup, I quickly closed my eyes and dumped everything into the sink. I don't know how I thought to skip the tap water and start with my heated up hot water first, then steep my tea separately and finally, dump the infused tea (chamomille) into the gelatin. That worked much better! So I first put 1 tablespoon of gelatin in a cup, poured just enough hot water from my tea kettle to cover it, mixed vigorously with a spoon until it was mixed with no clumps. Then I brewed my tea and when the tea was ready, I dumped the tea in the liquified gelatin, mixed some more, and drank quickly before it cooled down (as I was not about to find out what will happen if the tea cooled). I later on tried with hot chocolate, but I did not like the slimy-ish texture of the hot cocoa once it was mixed in. I don't know why the tea didn't get slimy-ish but the cocoa did. I had it today with green tea and it was even better than the chamomille.

So, here's the GOOD news for me (sorry you had to go through the technicalities before hand): less than 30 minutes after I had the gelatin-hot chocolate (that's actually what I made first, and then I decided to try it in tea to see if it will look better, which it did for me), my hip hurt much much less and I was not limping around the house anymore! I could still feel it, but it was 90% better!!! UPS came after 7:30 pm. By 8pm, I had the first 'dose' and I was almost painless before 8:20!! It says on the box to take 2 tablespoons a day, one in the morning and one at night (something like that). Well, before I went to bed (around 10 pm), I decided to have my second tablespoon (and that's when I brewed chamomille tea instead)! I slept well for the first time since around 6 weeks pregnant! I need to get a body pillow, and was buying time by just using my regular pillows in the house. Still, my hips will ache throughout the night, my back will get sore because it's not well supported, and I because I haven't been able to eat well, I would wake up very hungry in the middle of the night, try to get apple sauce or yogurt down and then, I wouldn't be able to go back to sleep. Last night for the first time in at least 2 months, I went to sleep, woke up twice to use the bathroom, but went right back to sleep, and the icing on the cake: MY HIP WAS COMPLETELY PAIN FREE! I pressed on it, I pressed on various parts of my bottom that would normally hurt if I did that but nothing, no pain!!! I slept till 8 (yay for winter break!) this morning and woke up STILL pain free!!! So I tried the gelatin in some yogurt (I was very hungry and didn't feel up to trying gelatin-cocoa or tea on an empty finicky pregnant stomach) and managed to eat 2/3 of it but I did not care at all for how it made the yogurt gelatin like (and it clumped some so it was not great at all). I didn't want to waste my cup of yogurt so I made myself eat most of it, waited 30 minutes or so and had some gelatin-cocoa (for calories and while I figured out what food sounded OK, as I have had a rough time eating). I was able to clean my 3 bedroom house, clean the bathroom, and do some laundry, all of these activities pain free!!! At around 4 pm, I took another tablespoon (in the green tea this time, and that's my favorite out the 3 --hot cocoa, chamomile tea and green tea) just because :-). And at 9:30, I took another tablespoon in chamomile tea before settling down to write this review and then go to bed. :-) I wondered if it's OK to take 3 tablespoons like I've done, and I might just stick to 2 a day like the box suggests...

What I am praying for: that this will heal my body as it changes to accommodate my pregnancy. That my hips and other joints, spine, ankles, knees will be in tip top shape as I take this throughout the pregnancy. That it will strengthen my muscles (does it do things for muscles??), especially my back and core stomach muscles, so that I will not strain them as baby grows, and that those parts actually heal stronger than ever after baby comes sometime in late spring!

Another incredible thing that happened was that I get what I think are varicose veins on the back of my thighs and they can get painful! Well, when I got home from work yesterday, I had a couple that popped on the back of my right thigh. By the time I was going to bed, they were almost gone, and I had to feel my skin with my bare hand to feel them! Normally, I KNOW they are there, and I can feel their bulge through my leggins! Today, they were still gone, but by evening time, I could feel them aching a bit. I still had to feel my skin to find them (at their usual place) but they were not as bulgy as usual, and I wonder if getting tired (I was physically busier today around the house than I have been able to be in two months, I was just so out of it) caused them to appear. Not medical at all, I know, and I will get them checked at my next visit to confirm whatever it is they are. But I am hoping that's something else the gelatin is taking care of for me!!!

Thanks for reading, hope this helps someone and gives someone hope for things they're hoping will heal or improve if they take gelatin. I'll try to come back and update as time goes on and I have more results!
top_rant.title: Seeing some results within a few minutes!!!

top_rant.text:
I got this [[ASIN:B08CBY1M9Q Kevin's Natural Foods Roasted Garlic Chicken Breast]] on sale from Whole Foods, after I’d previously tried (and LOVED!) [[ASIN:B083VKRV94 Kevin's Natural Foods Korean BBQ Chicken Breast]] when it was also on sale. However, at the time I bought the [[ASIN:B083VKRV94 Korean BBQ]] I only got one, because I’d never had it before and I didn’t want to risk being stuck with a lot of it, in case it turned out that I didn’t like it.

But I actually LOVED it, and I was planning to get more the next time that I shopped at Whole Foods. Unfortunately, it was no longer on sale the next time, and as far as I know, it has not been on sale since (nevertheless, I did get the Korean BBQ beef version on sale from Costco!) though this last time I shopped at Whole Foods, I noticed that several OTHER Kevin’s Natural Foods entrées were on sale. Seeing as how the one I LOVED was NOT on sale, I decided to take that chance to experiment/try the other varieties to see if I liked those as much or even more than the Korean BBQ. So after discarding the varieties that I knew or thought would be too spicy for me (I get heartburn & acid reflux among other issues, one of the reasons I don't like/can't tolerate spicy-hot food) including those labeled as “Medium” spice, like the Kung Pao Chicken or Chicken Tikka Masala (especially given that in many languages & dialects in India, the word “Tikka” or its variations [like “Thikat” in Marathi, or “Theek” in Konkani, my family’s mother tongue] itself means “spicy”!) the only remaining Kevin's Natural Foods entrées that were on sale were THIS [[ASIN:B08CBY1M9Q Roasted Garlic Chicken Breast]], a [[ASIN:B09B8JZ545 Chicken Breast Piccata]] and a [[ASIN:B08CBYRJHT Cilantro Lime Chicken Breast]]. Now, to be honest, I don’t care much for cilantro — and in fact, until recently I actually HATED (capitalized emphasis on HATE!) cilantro & couldn’t stand eating anything with cilantro in it, despite being of Indian descent, and cilantro being very common/frequently used in Indian cuisine which often made eating Indian food made by others (like when visiting family friends or relatives) more complicated indeed. It it was only within the last couple years or so, when my brother started making burgers with a combination of cilantro, curly parsley & shallots all chopped up really fine and mixed together inside of the burgers that I started developing a tolerance of (if not a taste for!) cilantro, at least in certain uses.Nevertheless, I still usually do NOT like cilantro, and both because of that AND because, as far as I know, the ingredients in Kevin’s Natural Foods items are NOT organic, and cilantro (the majority if not ALL herbs and greens, actually!) is among the most heavily pesticide-sprayed produce, I prefer to avoid non-organic whenever/wherever possible. As such, the only varieties of Kevin’s Natural Foods entrées on sale that were left, were the [[ASIN:B08CBY1M9Q Roasted Garlic Chicken Breast]], and the [[ASIN:B09B8JZ545 Chicken Breast Piccata]], and that is what I got. One of each, to try. But after eating the [[ASIN:B08CBY1M9Q Roasted Garlic Chicken Breast]] I have to say that I am NOT impressed. Not only that, I am actually pretty DISAPPOINTED!

First of all, UNLIKE the Korean BBQ chicken, in which the sous-vide chicken pieces separated pretty easily with the little massaging as directed in the instructions, the pieces in THIS [[ASIN:B08CBY1M9Q Roasted Garlic Chicken Breast]] were all clumped together. Despite massaging it for several minutes, it still remained stuck together. So, since the pan was starting to get hot, I had to just put it on the pan as is and when I tried to separate the pieces with the cooking spoon/spatula, little teeny bits/crumbs of chicken started breaking off everywhere. Yet,, I was still unable to break apart some of the pieces which resulted in some of the chicken “crumbs” getting overcooked and the bigger pieces/chunks barely being browned at all. On top of that, even though this is supposed to be a ROASTED GARLIC chicken breast, it was INCREDIBLY BLAND, with little to no real flavor. Now at least if it had mushrooms, or other ingredients, like mushrooms or vegetables to balance it out and give it a bit more flavor & texture, it’d be better. But there wasn’t. It was JUST the chicken and the sauce and THAT’S IT!

Which is why, I ended up adding a can of some organic mushroom pieces on my own. I also mixed in some more chicken, because after I’d put in the mushrooms, the amount of chicken became disproportionate. So I added several strips from a package of [[ASIN:B07NRC7THL Whole Foods Market Organic Grilled Chicken Breast Strips]], and then because the amount of sauce included in the package was naturally no longer sufficient for the additional ingredients, I added some [[ASIN:B06ZZLTVCW Kalona SuperNatural Organic Whipping Cream]] to increase the quantity enough to coat the additional chicken and the mushrooms. I also added some more garlic (actually, organic garlic powder) as well as pink Himalayan salt and a bit of black pepper to improve the flavor — and while all of that DID make this dish *somewhat* better, overall it was a HUGE LET-DOWN just the same, and in my opinion, even on sale for $7.19 marked down from $10.99, it was still definitely NOT worth the price. I could make a just-as-good (if not BETTER!) garlic chicken, that UNLIKE this dish would be with 100% ORGANIC ingredients, at a fraction of the cost of [[ASIN:B08CBY1M9Q Kevin's Natural Foods Roasted Garlic Chicken Breast]]. Granted, since my garlic chicken would not be “sous-vide” (at least not until I get a sous-vide machine), the chicken itself might not be as tender and juicy as sous-vide chicken, especially if using chicken breast. But it’d likely have more flavor, include more than just chicken and sauce, and be cheaper than [[ASIN:B08CBY1M9Q Kevin's Natural Foods Roasted Garlic Chicken Breast]] and if the [[ASIN:B09B8JZ545 Chicken Breast Piccata]], turns out to be equally disappointing, then I’ll probably just stick with the Korean BBQ one.

Honestly, there were only 2 reasons why I got this [[ASIN:B08CBY1M9Q Roasted Garlic Chicken Breast]] to begin with…………….……..the first was because it was on sale, and since I LOVED the Korean BBQ chicken dish, I wanted to try the company’s other dishes without having to pay the regular, outrageously-high (in my opinion, at least!) non-sale price, and the second reason was for convenience. I’m disabled (actually legally certified as permanently disabled!) and struggle with several chronic health issues/conditions (thanks a LOT, Big pHARMa!🤬) and on my “bad days”, I just don’t have the energy or stamina (or “the spoons” as its referred to in the disability and chronic illness/invisible illness community) to do much more than get out of bed and heat up pre-made stuff. So I got these entrées in order to have to make it for a “bad day” when I didn’t feel like doing a lot of cooking & especially prep work (cleaning & cutting up the chicken, etc.) and just wanted something quick, and simple for dinner. Because basically all you have to do to make it, is break up the chicken pieces, warm them up on the stove or in the microwave, mix in the sauce and warm that up as well and its done. Then if you want, heat up some noodles or rice to go with it. I like [[ASIN:B07WX13RX5 Annie Chun's Organic Hokkien Stir-Fry Noodles]] though that’d probably go better with Korean BBQ or other Asian inspired entrées. Personally, since I made this [[ASIN:B08CBY1M9Q Roasted Garlic Chicken Breast]] on a bad day that wasn’t as bad as others, I actually DID have enough energy to also make some pasta (specifically Kroger’s Simple Truth Organic brand egg noodles) to have with this entrée. But any pasta or noodles, or rice would probably be a decent option to complete the meal, especially if mushrooms and/or other vegetables were added to the chicken to make the dish more substantial.

Unfortunately, aside from the relative convenience of these entrées, all in all, this particular dish was a HUGE flop with me. But I gave it 2 stars instead of 1 star, only because it IS pretty easy to make — and like with the Korean BBQ chicken, the sous-vide makes the chicken breast really tender & juicy a lot more than chicken breast usually is. That is one of the reasons I am also planning to get a sous-vide machine hopefully in the near future, so I can make sous-vide using organic ingredients at a fraction of the cost of these non-organic boxed entrées. Regardless, I will likely NOT be buying THIS [[ASIN:B08CBY1M9Q Roasted Garlic Chicken Breast]] again since it was TOTALLY DISAPPOINTING!

 ⭐️⭐️ 2 STARS!!!!!!!!!!!
top_rant.title: Meh….....…..….Nowhere Near as Good as the Korean BBQ — and Definitely NOT Worth Even the Sale Price!

top_rant.text:
[[VIDEOID:4e60a534962cd560960dcf143fc7b6db]] I loved the idea of this scratch off date chart. As someone who doesn’t get out much (and often prefers it that way), who is dating someone who DOES like to go out, I wanted to put something in place that would motivate me to get out more for him. As a completionist who loves a good challenge, this chart seemed like JUST the thing to do it!

This scratch off date chart seems to be high enough quality, made of thick, glossy paper and packaged in a protective tube with tissue paper to prevent scratching of the little hearts. It has space to write yours and your partners names on it at the bottom as well. Once I got it unrolled, I laid it flat on the kitchen counter with my laptop on it overnight and that did a fantastic job of making it not want to roll up on itself anymore. It was now flat, so I could hang it up.

I must say though, that the hearts are not just pink, they are PINK! In your face NEON PINK. That was a bit of a surprise and made it less appealing to hang in a public space of my home so it was relegated to the door of the guest room closet. I think they could improve the look to be a bit classier.

It comes with a little pink ‘guitar’ pick for scratching off the hearts as you complete your dates, however, there is no way to store the pick with the chart. This could be improved by including a little adhesive Velcro square with the set, which I had on hand and worked perfectly. The corners of the chart are already pink so I velcroid the pick to a corner and it’s hardly noticeable and won’t get lost.

Now for my real grievances…
I didn’t notice all the grammatical errors on the chart until it arrived. This is probably not a big deal to most people but it bugged ME to death! It’s like no one bothered to proof it before printing! I tried to illustrate this in my photos but to name a few:
-Two, and ONLY two of the dates have a period at the end of them. Why???
- One of the dates uses the word ‘too’ where it should be ‘to’.
- Some use the letter ‘a’ and some don’t. I don’t care either way as long as it’s consistent, but this chart is all over the place. ‘go to A museum’, ‘go to coffee shop’, ‘take A walk’, ‘take scenic drive’, ‘go to A scenic point’... you get the idea.

I also couldn’t really see all the titles in the photo and they aren’t listed in the description… I have typed them all out and am including them in a list below.

Many of the suggestions are not even dates; more like ideas for stuff to do together when you are bored. A great deal of them don’t even require leaving your house. I don’t hate that, it’s just, they aren’t dates…. This should be called a ‘couples ACTIVITY chart’ because if a guy asked me out and then told me we’d be playing mad libs all evening I would think he was nuts… lolol… That doesn’t bother me so much though, but what DOES bother me, is that a few of them are not even activities that most couples would WANT or BE WILLING to do together. Visiting family? Getting matching tattoos? Going to the dog pound? Are they nuts? These are NOT DATES!

Furthermore, a few of the date suggestions are almost identical to others, or too vague/confusing to define what is meant by them. What is a ‘goodwill’ date? There is already a ‘volunteer’ date and a ‘thrift shopping’ date, so what’s the difference? And what is a ‘faith-night in’ supposed to mean?? Aren’t going out for ice cream and going out for frozen yogurt just the same date with less flavor???

This really bothers me the most because it makes this chart further appear as though not much thought was put into it. To be PERFECTLY honest, I would lean towards believing that more time and thought went into writing this review (which took me several hours over a couple days) than the creation of this chart. I mean, there are so many OBVIOUS date suggestions they don’t include on this poster (that are also better than so many that they do) there is NO need for duplicates. For example, go camping, go on a horse and carriage ride, build a fort, go gem mining, visit a botanical garden, go on a hot air balloon ride, tour a vineyard or brewery, rent paddleboats…. Why is VISITING A RELATIVE on this list as a DATE?

Not all the items on this list are going to be for everyone and certainly many couples will choose not to do certain things.
My boyfriend and I chose to print little labels and replace the ones that were not for us (all the sports, for starters) with activities WE enjoy, like comicons and being creative. So with a side yard of bamboo at my disposal, I added ‘build a teepee’ to our chart along with ‘complete an upcycled pallet project together’ – stuff like that - because that’s US. It would actually be nice if a matching few blank stickers were included to make hand written changes to the chart if you want… ours is looking pretty ratchet at this point. rofl

It turned into half the chart getting changed, and we left the rest wide open to interpretation. Lol
So in addition to a list of the included dates, I am ALSO going to list all the things we came up with to replace the things we didn’t want to do, and any tweaks to the dates we thought were not clear enough or needed to adjusted to be more relevant to us.

Instead of listing them in the order they appear on the chart, I am also going to attempt to group them together by style of date, and/or similarity, to further illustrate how many repeated activities there are, and how many sports they want you to play together and different kinds of shopping they want you to do and styles of food they want you to eat. Half the chart is easting, playing sports and shopping. LOL

INCLUDED ON THIS POSTER ARE THE FOLLOWING DATES:

GO TO AN ART FESTIVAL

HAVE A BARBEQUE
HOST A DINNER PARTY

GO TO AN ANIMAL SHELTER (whyyyy? To be sad? To accidentally adopt a pet together? changed to ‘Animal Date’. Our town has 2 interactive farms. One that does yoga and movie nights with baby goats and another that does picnics with alpacas… lol but this could also include having a date that is centered around your pets)
GO TO A WILDLIFE RESERVE
GO TO THE ZOO

GO TO A COLLEGE SPORTS GAME

PLAY TENNIS

GO TO THE BATTING CAGES
WORKOUT TOGETHER
GO ON A 5K RUN (changed to ‘participate in a charity walkathon / fun run like colorrun or night nation)
GO TO A DRIVING RANGE
GO ICE SKATING
GO ROLLER SKATING
PLAY LASER TAG
GO TO A TRAMPOLINE PARK
GO HORSEBACK RIDING

GO SWIMMING
GO TO THE LAKE
GO TO A WATER PARK

VISIT A NEW TOWN
EXPLORE NEW CITIES

GO ON A DOUBLE DATE

FAITH NIGHT IN (WHAT DOES THIS EVEN MEAN? WATCH CHURCH ON TV OR READ TO EACH OTHER FROM THE BIBLE DATE?)

GET MATCHING TATTOOS (um no!?)

WRITE A POEM TO EACH OTHER
WRITE A SONG TOGETHER

MAKE A SCRAPBOOK TOGETHER (good luck getting your man to do this! However, mine loves caricatures and wants it to be a thing for us. I said I’d never hang them on the wall but I do love getting them done and think they make cute fun memories… but they always end up rolled up somewhere on a shelf or in a box. HE actually suggested making a scrapbook for them before we even got this date poster. So I guess just be creative with it and he might participate? lol)

VOLUNTEER SOMEWHERE
GO ON A GOODWILL DATE NIGHT (we changed this to a ‘random acts of kindness’ date)

MAKE A DIY PROJECT

HAVE A PHOTO SHOOT (we have 3 dogs and 3 ducks between us and chose to have a pet shoot for this one and dress our babies up in star trek cosplay. This also covered the DIY project since we will make the duck’s costumes ahead of time on a different day. LoL)

YOUR DREAM DATE!

Now obviously there isn’t a person out there who will love EVERY SINGLE suggestion on that list, so what I did was print two copies of the date list above, and two copies of the additional date ideas I came up with below, and my boyfriend and I both gave each idea a rating of 1-5 of how much we liked it.
1) HARD PASS. No way.
2) I’d rather not, but I mean…
3) Neutral. I’m down if it makes you happy…
4) Could be fun. I’ll try anything once.
5) HECK YEAH! Let’s do it!

Anything with a 1 on either of our lists was replaced immediately, no questions asked, and I also had plenty of ideas left to replace the 2s also. We found that discussing the 3s often led to them becoming 4s or even 5s when clarified or tweaked! LoL for example: Camping with no bathroom is a HARD PASS 1 for me, but he loves camping. Camping WITH a bathroom is a 3/neutral on my scale, but GLAMPING… well… THAT is a 4-5 sure why not! Ha ha
I found this step to also be a great way to get to know him better as we are a rather new couple.

THIS IS A LIST OF REPLACEMENT DATE IDEAS I CAME UP WITH FOR OUR CHART:
Go Camping/Glamping
Go on a Horse & Carriage Ride
Build a Fort
Go Gem Mining
Visit a Botanical Garden
Participate in a Murder Mystery Dinner
Go Randonauting
Build a Gingerbread House
Go Geocaching
Attend a BYOB Paint Night
Take a Dance Class
Attend a Black Tie Event (like a gala or a second chance prom)
Participate in a Fun Run
Get Mani/Pedis Together
Go to a Bookstore
Rent Paddleboats/Kayaks
Get a Couples Massage
Plan a Stupidly Romantic Evening In
Stay at a Unique Air B&B
Go Axe Throwing
Visit a Planetarium
Have a Date with Animals
Participate in a Scavenger Hunt
Go on an App Assisted Adventure Date
Go on a Bar Crawl
Visit a Pottery Painting Studio
Go Apple Picking
Solve a Corn Maze
Go on a Ghost Walk
Visit a Haunted House
Try Yoga
Attend an Event in Costume/Cosplay
Watch a Movie Outdoors
Go See Fireworks
Play Truth or Dare

So. Here we are at the summary of this review… the big question looming before us… Is this poster worth it? I still say yes… IF… you are a homebody like me who doesn’t like to go out or try new things, but ARE a completionsist who also doesn’t mind putting in some effort to make edits to the chart… then it’s a great way to get you out there on the town!

However, I DO think the list could be VERY much improved upon and while I haven’t explored other options, it might be worth looking around because there is a good chance that a better chart exists.

top_rant.text:
This is going to be a long review but here it goes! All of these products are available on Amazon
1. The lilies sour worms are really good. They are almost identical to the project seven worms. They are fairly sour, but not overly so. They have interesting flavors, but the bags are overwhelmingly passion fruit flavor. There is an apple peach flavor, but I get almost none. They are low in calories at 70 calories per bag and have a nice portion! They are similarly priced to the other sour candies. I’m not gonna really mention price from here on out, because they’re all pretty dang similar. They aren’t too sticky or too hard, the consistency is really good. They aren’t really chewy like a traditional sour worm, but I like that! They have 5net carbs per bag. They don’t stick to my teeth much at all.
2. Project 7 Sour Worms- these worms taste almost identical to the lilies, so identical that I think one might have taken the others formulas. They have the lowest calories at 60 per bag which I love, and they have a nice portion! They have lemon lime, passion fruit, and rainbow ice (my fav) flavor. They are sour, but not overly sour. Do use only natural flavorings. They have 6 net carbs per bag. These stick to my teeth a tiny bit, but not in a rip my fillings out kind of way
3. Project 7 Apple Rings- I absolutely love these, they taste a little more natural than a jolly rancher. The brand uses only natural flavorings. They are sour but not overly sour and they have a nice consistency, not too chewy. You get a really nice portion and they’re only 60 cal. They also have six net carbs and stick to my teeth a little.
Smart sweets peach rings- their original family formulation was one of my favorites, then they reformulated and they became hard as rocks & ripped my fillings out. Now it’s 2022, The reformulated again and they are softer which I really do like. They are very lightly sour. They stick to my teeth a little bit but it’s fine. These have a whopping 110 cal a bag which I really don’t like. You get a decent size portion, they have a whopping 23 net carbs.
4. Pink panda Astro blasters: These are very very sour candies, maybe overly so. I buy them and sprinkle a pack of equal on them to sweeten them up . Then I love them. If you ever get a sour candy that’s too sour, just just some sweetener of your choice on them and they will be delicious (you’re already eating a bag of artificial/ low cal sweetener anyway !) They are barely sweet. They have a nice traditional flavor selection: lemon, lime, cherry orange, apple, pear . They have 90 cal a bag and you get a nice selection. They have 16 net carbs.
5. Pink panda peachy hearts- these are barely sour. They are a nice meaty candy, which I enjoy. They have a different flavor peach and the smart sweets. They are 90 cal a bag and you get a nice portion. They have 16 net carbs.
6. Smart sweets Sour Melon - these are fairly sour, not as sour as the Astro blasters but pretty up there. They are also a little bit sweet. They have an interesting consistency and stick to my teeth a little bit. People say these taste like acetone but I just disagree. They have 100 cal and you get a pretty decent portion.
7. Smart sweets Sour Blast Buddies – these are probably my least favorite of this review. There’s something that taste off of them and they have a strange texture. They really taste fake. They are the wrong texture for a gummy, like a starburst but WORSE. They are very sour and barely sweet. They have a lot of citrus in there and red was underwhelming, if you are more of a red and white person, I don’t recommend these, as their citrus is very strong and reminds me of straight up lemon. They have 100 cal and 18 carbs
8. Project 7 Sour Fruit Gummies- these are very sour. They only have 60 cal a bag and 6 net carbs. You get a good portion. They are just a little bit chewy and stick to your teeth but barely. I love project seven other sour candies so I tend to stick to those. But I’m thinking of ordering another pack of these.
9. Smashgummy Pucker up-
These are chronically out of stock and on some days they are my favorites, but my favorite vaires all the time. They have two servings in a bag, so the amount is gigantic but the calories are only 120 for both servings. They have my favorite flavor selection which includes raspberry. These have the perfect consistency slightly chewy but not too chewy. They are a nice balance of sour and sweet. They appear more expensive, but they’re not because you get two servings a bag. They are 3 net carbs per serving, or 6 net carbs per bag. They are just barely sticky close to not at all.
10. Project 7 Sour Straws
Another chronically out of stock one that I just love. They are these big meaty straws and they have the best lemonade flavor. They also have a watermelon flavor. They are a little sticky and a little firmer, but not so much that it hurts to chew them or they rip my fillings out. They are not overly sour, I’d say about as sour as smart sweets peach rings, maybe a hair more. They have a nice sweetness and they kind of remind me of fruit roll ups. They have 100 cal and 11 net carbs. I feel like you get a little more candy in this, but they have a little more calories.
Honorable mention
11. Smart sweets gummy worms – these are NOT SOUR but since they were worms I thought I’d throw them in here. I really love these. I love the consistency. They really have the texture of a real gummy worm. They don’t stick to your teeth AT ALL, so if you have a difficult filling, you could eat these. They are 100 cal a bag and the portion is a little smaller and other smart sweets. My only complaint is that the flavor is mild and I’d like them to be more acidic. I still find myself eating them all the time.
12. Smart owl watermelon slices
Wow these are new! LOVE THEM! A nice sour watermelon that reminds me a bit of natural watermelon & a bit of a charms watermelon, the perfect marriage! They are the perfect level of sour,which is pretty sour but also sweet. However they are not overly sweet. They are a nice meaty candy. Smart owl is pink panda’s reformation and I love them all! They are vegan & have 90 calories a bag. The portion is a hair smaller then others, but I think that’s because each candy is so large, so it’s just enough to be satisfied. They are barely sticky and on the softer side for still being chewy. I love the texture. They have a whopping 18 carbs.
13. Smart owl peach - these look just like those Haribo two toned peach discs, *so pretty*,just what you want in a candy. They are medium sour and have a great peach flavor different then smart sweets and pink panda. It reminds me of real peaches in a meaningful way. They are vegan and have 90 calories. They have 17 net carbs. These are not too soft, not too chewy, and a little bit sticky. I love that this and the watermelon slices come in a variety pack!!! If you are a sour lover, grab it!
14. Snack owl sour nova’s these are pink pans is reformulation of the Astro blasters. They are pretty sour and barely sweet. They are a little too sour for my liking, but what I do is I sprinkle a little sweetener on top of them and then I love it. They have a nice flavor selection blue raspberry, black, cherry, lime, lemon, orange, and tropical punch. They have a medium chew, not too chewy. They stick to my teeth only a little. They are 90 cal per bag and you get a nice standard portion. They have 19 net carbs.
15. Shameless Snacks Apple wow I just discovered these! These are medium sour and fairly sweet, which I love! They have a great consistency, where they are not too chewy and barely sticky. But they are just chewy enough to enjoy them. They have an unbelievable apple flavor like a real granny Smith, mixed with a jolly rancher. I’ve ordered two boxes of these in two weeks! They are shaped like a sour patch kid. They are unbelievably keto friendly with only 3 net carbs. They give you a really nice portion for only 70 calories!
16. Shameless Snacks Raspberry: love this raspberry candy that is shaped like a sour patch kid. They are mediums, shower, and fairly sweet! They have medium-soft chew that is perfect! They taste like if you made raspberry jam into a candy. They only have 3 net carbs per bag and 70 calories, I’m obsessed! They also have a nice sized portion and they barely stick to my teeth
17. Shameless Snacks Watermelon these are really good like the other two shameless snacks. They have the same shape and texture with that medium soft chew 70 calories 3 net carbs. They have a nice watermelon flavor but I prefer the snack owl watermelon & the kiss my keto watermelon( not sour) to these. If I didn’t have those others I’d love these. They reminded me of a sour watermelon charms pop. They a lightly sour. They barely stick to my teeth
18. Shameless Snacks Blue Raspberry again, these have the same shape and consistency as the other shameless snacks, a light medium chew, 70 cal, nice portion, 3 net carbs, not too sticky, medium soft consistency. I had an issue with this flavor because it doesn’t taste like blue raspberry which I freakin love. I looked at the ingredients and flavors were listed as such: cherry, banana, & pineapple flavors. It tastes like a mix of those 3 flavors which I’m sure many will love, but it isn’t blue raspberry! Still they are good but not for me.

top_rant.text:
Ever since I first discovered this yogurt, [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie, Smooth, Plain Whole Milk Yogurt]] it has not only been pretty much the ONLY yogurt my family buys, but also something that is bought pretty much EVERY TIME we shop at Whole Foods (since it seems to be the only local store in a reasonable radius that carries this brand AND variety) EXCEPT for if/when its sold out or out of stock, in which case, unless the supply of yogurt at home is enough to last a week (or at least several days) until the next shopping trip, then the only choice is to get whatever brand of plain yogurt, preferably organic, that they do have — and in fact, if I recall correctly, that was how I discovered [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie, Plain Whole Milk Yogurt]] in the first place!

Prior to that, ever since I "went organic" beginning in 2008-2009 or so, and stopped buying the [[ASIN:B00RASDV2E Dannon Plain Yogurt]], or when not an option (like being out of stock) the generic/store brand of non-organic plain yogurt that I (or my parents when I was younger) had been buying ever since I can remember, I started trying out different brands of organic plain yogurt to find the one we all liked best. Eventually, [[ASIN:B0793CP1K2 Kalona SuperNatural Organic Cream-Top Plain Yogurt]] was picked as the favorite, and either [[ASIN:B071YBL28J Maple Hill Creamery Organic Plain Yogurt]], or Stonyfield Organic Plain Yogurt OR [[ASIN:B074H7KKRV 365 Everyday Value Organic Plain Yogurt]] as a back-up if the [[ASIN:B0793CP1K2 Kalona SuperNatural]] was not available. But one day when I was getting groceries from Whole Foods, they were all OUT of the [[ASIN:B0793CP1K2 Kalona SuperNatural]] AS WELL AS the [[ASIN:B071YBL28J Maple Hill Creamery]], the Stonyfield Organic AND the [[ASIN:B074H7KKRV 365 Everyday Value Organic]] and since I had barely ½ a carton of yogurt left at home, and someone (and often, more than one!) in my family eats yogurt nearly every day, waiting until the next shopping trip to buy yogurt was not an option, and I was forced to find a substitute. But even if it necessitated looking in other stores, and no matter what the brand (or when it comes to what's most important to me, whether its whole milk, low-fat or nonfat, or even whether its organic or not), the one & only NON-NEGOTIABLE was/is that it has to be PLAIN (unsweetened) yogurt with NOTHING ELSE (like fruit, nuts, granola, candy, etc.) added to it.

Because, although my brother & I were born, raised and have lived our entire lives in the US, I'm/we're of Indian descent, being that my parents are immigrants from India (they came to the US in their 20s, during the late 1960s & early 1970s and have been here ever since) who became US citizens — and plain unsweetened yogurt, also called "curds" (as well as "dahi" or "dhai" in Marathi-speaking and Konkani-speaking families [which my family are!] & maybe others), is pretty much a kitchen staple in the vast majority, maybe even ALL, Indian and Indian-descent households, just like rice. Although Indian people and especially people of Indian descent in the US and other countries, DO eat flavored and/or sweetened yogurt. at times, such as frozen yogurt, for dessert, the FACT is that plain unsweetened yogurt is not only eaten as an accompaniment to most, or even EVERY meal, but it's also an integral part of so many entrées and snacks (www dot tinyurl dot com/curdsdishes) from regions ALL over India.

For example, plain unsweetened yogurt is one of the primary ingredients in "dahi-khadi", a yogurt-based curry originating from the Indian state of Rajasthan, and "Doi Macch", a Bengali & Oriya dish of fish in a yogurt curry, as well as "dahi-vada", a popular Indian snack of fried spiced flour balls in a spiced yogurt-based sauce, and "raita", a chopped salad with a yogurt dressing. Not to mention that, it is literally the MAIN ingredient in "dahi-bhaath", a yogurt & rice dish dish popular in the South Indian states of Kerala (where its known as "tayire chōre"), Tamil Nadu (where it's "thayir saadam"), Karnataka (where its called "mosaranna"), Telangana & Andhra Pradesh (where its "perugannam" or "daddojanam") as well as Maharashtra (where its known as "dahi bhat" or "dahi-bhaath") and its a staple of traditional cuisine, with the untempered spice version present at the end of almost every South Indian meal, and the tempered-spice version is often served during formal occasions. In addition, the tempered-spice version is also offered to devotees in temples as "prasadam" or "prasad", which is Hinduism-approved (so, only lacto-vegetarian and typically "sattvic" [www dot tinyurl dot com/sattvics] instead of "tamasic" [www dot tinyurl dot com/tamasic] food & drink — meaning no meat, poultry, seafood, eggs, garlic or anything from the onion [onions, chives, shallots, leeks, etc.] or mushroom/fungi family) food and/or drink that has been blessed by a Hindu priest, or informally (like at home), first, by being placed at/in front of the altar of Hindu deities, as an offering to them, and then consumed as holy food, after prayers. "Dahi-bhaath" is also often packed in "tiffins" (metal [usually stainless steel] lunchboxes) as travel food to be eaten during train or bus journeys in lieu of buying food en route.

I have such fond memories from childhood/adolescence of going to India, and traveling by overnight train (which usually left in the late afternoon or early evening, and arrived around 6 or 7 am the next morning) to various cities to visit relatives — and at least on my 2nd visit to India at 7 (during my 1st visit to India, I was only 3, so even if we HAD traveled by trains then, I don't have ANY concrete memory of it!) the excitement & novelty of the sleeper cars, and in particular, the upper berths/beds that folded up & down from the wall, which, up to that point, I had never seen before, at least as far as I can remember. My brother & I had an ABSOLUTE BALL playing on the upper berths of the sleeper cabin, pretending that "the floor is hot lava" and trying (not always so successfully!😉) to jump across the cabin from one upper bunk to the other...................and especially (if not most!) memorable was the food eaten & beverages drunk on those train journeys in India. Not only the food/drink themselves, but all the experiences & vivid memories that go along with it. Like the refreshment seller(s) who'd walk down the aisle of the train car (which separated the cabins on either side of the train) hawking his sandwiches (which he called out [in a shouting voice] SANDWEECH!), hot chai, coffee and other foods & drinks, like a carnival act, or a peddler at a street fair, or market/bazaar. Which is why even though relatives usually packed the aforementioned "dahi-bhaath" as well as fruit, bottled water (because obviously being from the US, we couldn't drink regular water in India) & other snacks for us to eat on the journey, sometimes we still bought stuff from the refreshment seller(s), even if only hot coffee or chai, or a cold soda pop to accompany the food. To this day, years (actually DECADES!) later, I still remember the "dahi-bhaath" in stainless steel "tiffins" and how great it all tasted. Especially with hot chai/coffee or better yet, a cold [[ASIN:B00N3JZP5K Thums Up]] cola or [[ASIN:B00N3KI5O2 Limca]] lemon-lime soda in a glass bottle to drink with it while riding trains in India. Not just during the 2-week (or so) visit when I was 7, but also the much longer trips (when a LOT more sightseeing in India was done) of a 3 month summer vacation at 12, and 5 month stay at 17.

But I digress, though the intent was to emphasize how essential plain unsweetened yogurt is, and its central/essential role in Indian cuisine. In other words, plain unsweetened yogurt is a necessity, and as such, being without it (or at least without something similar [even if not ideal] like buttermilk or sour cream) is inconceivable, basically anathema to most, if not ALL people living in India or abroad, who are of Indian descent.

Now, as mentioned, one day when I was getting groceries at Whole Foods, the supply of plain yogurt at home was running really low, not enough to last until the next shopping trip. But, unfortunately (or fortunately, as it turned out!) on that particular day, it just happened that the store was sold out of ALL THREE of my usual brands ([[ASIN:B0793CP1K2 Kalona SuperNatural]] or in a pinch, either Stonyfield Organic OR [[ASIN:B074H7KKRV 365 Everyday Value Organic Plain]]) of plain yogurt — and out of the only organic whole-milk plain yogurts that they DID have in stock, we had tried all but one, and had not really liked any of them. The ONLY brand I had never tried before (I don't know why I'd never tried it before, but for whatever reason, I hadn't) was this [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie, Plain Whole Milk Yogurt]]. So rather than either buying a brand I knew I or my family didn't really like, OR having the hassle of trying another store, OR having back to the store in a couple days or so just for the yogurt, I just decided to give the [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic]] yogurt a chance................and I'm glad I did, because I and my family LOVE it. So much so that it has pretty much become our favorite yogurt, and unless its out of stock (in which case, we default to [[ASIN:B0793CP1K2 Kalona SuperNatural]], or [[ASIN:B071YBL28J Maple Hill Creamery]], or Stonyfield Organic or [[ASIN:B074H7KKRV 365 Everyday Value Organic]]), [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic]] is the only brand of yogurt we buy now.

The first and foremost reason is that [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie Yogurt]] is DELICIOUS — and while, taste-wise, [[ASIN:B0793CP1K2 Kalona SuperNatural Organic]] is almost as good, if not JUST as good as [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie]], the FACT is that, at 20¢ an oz, the unit price of the [[ASIN:B0793CP1K2 Kalona SuperNatural Organic]] is the most out of ANY of the other brands of yogurt I buy (or have bought). It's a lot more expensive (at 6¢ more per ounce, which may not seem like much, but multiply that by a 24 oz container, and then multiply THAT by the 3-5 containers I usually buy) than [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie]]. Even the [[ASIN:B071YBL28J Maple Hill Creamery Organic Plain Yogurt]] is cheaper at 17¢ an oz, and of course, the generic Whole Foods brand, [[ASIN:B074H7KKRV 365 Everyday Value Organic Plain Yogurt]] is the cheapest at 10¢ an oz. But granted, the [[ASIN:B074H7KKRV 365 Everyday Value Organic]] yogurt definitely doesn't taste the same or anywhere near as good as the [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie]], (which at 14¢ an oz is the next cheapest), or the [[ASIN:B0793CP1K2 Kalona SuperNatural]], or [[ASIN:B071YBL28J Maple Hill Creamery]] yogurts. Which is why [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie]] has become our first choice of yogurt. It is smooth and creamy, and has just the right amount of tang, without being overwhelming.

Just to be clear though, this review is JUST for the PLAIN [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie]], and NOT any of the other varieties (like the [[ASIN:B00K1XKUYM Wallaby Organic Aussie Greek-Style]] which I also❤️ but being more expensive, I only buy on occasion), particularly the flavored and/or sweetened varieties which I have never had. In fact, with the exception of frozen yogurt, I don't think I've ever really eaten flavored and/or sweetened yogurt. I don't know why, I've just never gotten the appeal of yogurt with fruit or granola, nuts, or worst of all, CANDY or COOKIES mixed in. Maybe growing up eating "dahi"(plain yogurt) with or as one of the dishes of many/most meals, as a SAVORY accompaniment instead of sweet, I just never developed a taste for the flavored/sweetened yogurts. Nevertheless, [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie]] plain yogurt is still YUMMY as it is. In addition to the aforementioned (and other) Indian dishes that plain yogurt can be used in, along with being an accompaniment with Indian meals, this yogurt is just as GREAT on its own. Personally, I LOVE having this plain yogurt with just a bit of pink Himalayan salt and chives (fresh or dried) mixed in.

All in all, this [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie]] plain yogurt has become my FAVORITE, and in all likelihood, my family's too. My mom has raved about it on several occasions, and has some of it (often several spoons worth), pretty much every single day, saying its one of the best she's had. I honestly agree. Other than *maybe* Greek yogurt, I don't think that ANY of the other plain yogurt that I've had in my 40+ years of life in the US, are anywhere near as good. Whether its [[ASIN:B00RASDV2E Dannon]], or even whether its organic brands like [[ASIN:B0793CP1K2 Kalona SuperNatural]], or [[ASIN:B071YBL28J Maple Hill Creamery]], or Stonyfield or [[ASIN:B074H7KKRV 365 Everyday Value Organic]] or whether its generic non-organic varieties.............NONE of them compare to the GREAT taste of the [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie]] which truly reminds me of the homemade "dahi" that I always had at relatives homes in India. Because at the times I visited (don't know about now -- I haven't been to India since 1995-96) everyone always made their own yogurt at home instead of buying it in a store. To the best of my recollections this [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby]] yogurt was the first store-bought yogurt I've had in the US that manages to best replicate that taste.

In short, I ❤️ this [[ASIN:B0010XT5IE Wallaby Organic Aussie Smooth, Plain Whole Milk Yogurt]]!!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ 5 STARS!!!!!!!

JOLLY TIME'S BLAST O' BUTTER IS A POOR OFFERING FROM WHAT USED TO BE A TOP OF THE LINE POPCORN COMPANY. IT'S CHEWY, DEBATABLY STALE, AND WITH MORE "OLD MAIDS" THAN A BINGO CONVENTION IT'S TASTE IS FAR FROM BUTTERY WHILE ITS AFTERTASTE IS SPIT-IT-OUT MEDICINAL.. IT SADDENS ME TO WRITE SUCH A DISMAL REVIEW OF JOLLY TIME. FOR MORE THAN FORTY YEARS, BEFORE I WENT MICRO FOR THE EASE OF IT, FOLLOWING MY DAD'S LIFELONG CHOICE I BOUGHT ONLY JOLLY TIME WHITE CORN FOR POPPING. MY OWN TWO FAMILIES OF KIDS GREW UP ON IT WITH NEVER A COMPLAINT [ OR MORE THAN A MERE FEW "OLD MAID" KERNELS PER KETTLE.] AS MOST POPCORN LOVERS AGREE, MICROWAVE POPCORN PALES IN COMPARISON WITH SWEET-CREAM-BUTTER-DRIZZLED AND SEA SALTED HAND-MADE, AND WHILE I DON'T CONSIDER MYSELF A POPCORN CONNOSIEUR I CAN TELL THE GOOD FROM THE BAD AND THE UGLY. OF THE MAJOR BRANDS I'VE TRIED, JOLLY TIME'S BLAST O' BUTTER RATES A MONUMENTAL "UGLY" FROM ME. POP WEAVER'S BUTTERY PITY ISN'T MUCH BETTER, AND I SCORE IT SIMPLY "BAD"... TO SAY IT'S KINDA SOGGY AND TASTES AS IF DUPONT CHEMICALS MARKETED IT WOULD BE A KINDNESS. MOVING ON TO #3 OF 5, DESPITE ITS HIGHER PRICE AND NATIONAL NAME ORVILLE REDENBACHERS' LINE OF FLUFFIES IS A LETDOWN TALLYING BARELY MIDDLIN' "GOOD" FOR TASTE AND CRUNCHINESS. A MUCH BETTER CORN, PERCHED ON THE NEXT RUNG HIGHER, IS ACT II'S MOVIE THEATER STYLE. BY MY LIKEABILITY INDEX THIS BRAND'S ONLY [ MINOR ] DRAWBACKS ARE ITS PUNY 2.5 OZ. BAG SIZE. AND TENDENCY TO BURN IF YOU'RE NOT VIGILANT. MY FAVORITE, SATISFYING EVERY MICROWAVE POPCORN CATEGORY WITH AN A+ GRADE IS POP SECRET'S MOVIE THEATER VARIETY. WELL, THAT'S MY TALESAY AND I'M STICKIN' TO IT.

top_screaming.text:
THE COMPANY STATES ON THEIR PRODUCT LABEL THAT THE HONEY IS RAW AND ORGANIC. WHEN I REQUESTED INFORMATION DIRECTLY FROM THE COMPANY AS TO WHO THEY WERE WORKING WITH IN BRAZIL-- (WHICH IS THE COUNTRY OF ORIGIN FOR THIS PRODUCT)-- TO ENSURE THAT IT WAS INDEED MEETING THE USDA ORGANIC CERTIFICATION STANDARDS, THE COMPANY REFUSED TO GIVE ME ANY INFORMATION AS TO WHOM THEY WERE USING AS A CERTIFICATION AGENCY IN BRAZIL. THEY INSTEAD REFERRED ME TO A 3RD PARTY CERTIFICATION AGENCY IN THE U.S.--QAI--WHO DO NOT HAVE ANY REPRESENTATIVE AGENTS IN BRAZIL TO DO THE CERTIFICATION! I CONTACTED THEM TO VERIFY THIS.

FOR THOSE NOT FAMILIAR WITH THIS WHOLE USDA ORGANIC CERTIFICATION PROCESS -FOR FOOD PRODUCTS FROM OTHER COUNTRIES--HOW IT WORKS IS THAT ANY COMPANY THAT WANTS TO BRING IN A PRODUCT FROM A FOREIGN COUNTRY AND SELL IT AS RAW AND ORGANIC... MEETING THE USDA ORGANIC STANDARDS...HAS TO WORK WITH A CERTIFICATION AGENCY THAT ACTUALLY HAS REPRESENTATIVES IN THAT COUNTRY PRODUCING THE PRODUCT. THESE CERTIFICATION AGENCIES ARE LISTED ON THE USDA ORGANIC WEBSITE PAGE. YOU TYPE IN THE COUNTRY YOU WANT TO CHECK ON, AND THE USDA WEBSITE PULLS UP A LIST OF ALL THE CERTIFIERS ACTUALLY LOCATED IN THAT COUNTRY THAT ARE APPROVED BY USDA TO OVERSEE AND APPROVE THE PRODUCTION OF THE FOOD PRODUCT SO AS TO MEET USDA ORGANIC STANDARDS.

ANY ONE OF THE USDA APPROVED CERTFIERS IN THAT COUNTRY (BRAZIL) THEN SUBMITS THEIR DOCUMENTATION TO THE COMPANY WHOSE PRODUCT THEY HAVE 'USDA ORGANIC' APPROVED. THEN, THAT COMPANY, IN TURN, SUBMITS IT TO A 3RD PARTY U.S. BASED CERTIFICATION AGENCY--IN THIS CASE QAI--AND QAI REVIEWS THE DOCUMENTS, DECIDING WHETHER TO STAMP ITS APPROVAL FOR USDA ORGANIC STANDARD.

FOR SOME, THIS WAY OF CERTIFICATION MAY SUFFICE. AS FOR ME, IT DOES NOT. I WOULD LIKE TO KNOW, AS A CONSUMER, WHO IN BRAZIL IS DIRECTLY INVOLVED IN OVERSEEING AND VALIDATING THE QUALITY OF A PRODUCT...ESPECIALLY WHEN YOU ARE DEALING WITH OTHER COUNTRIES... I AM DISAPPOINTED THAT THIS COMPANY WAS NOT MORE TRANSPARENT AND FORTHCOMING WITH THE INFORMATION I REQUESTED. THERE SHOULD BE A HIGHER STANDARD SET WHEN OFFERING WHOLESOME RAW ORGANICS PRODUCTS TO THE PUBLIC. CONSUMERS HAVE A RIGHT TO KNOW...AND I FEEL THAT I WAS BEING GIVEN A RUN AROUND...WHEN THE COMPANY RESPONDED TO MY CONCERNS, THEIR REPLY WAS EVASIVE, AND NOT SATISFACTORY.

I have tried many brands of quinoa, and after trying this one I stopped looking! It is the BEST there is!

Yes, it's more expensive than some... but in this case you get what you pay for. I tried buying some in bulk from a local Mediterranean store, and it was full of twigs and other crap, and needed endless cleaning and rinsing before it could be used. Who has time for that crap?

This quinoa requires no rinsing... and I have never found a single bit of foreign material in it, ever! I love cooking a batch of it with a chicken bouillon cube or two dissolved in the water. Having a batch handy in my fridge is useful for several days. I love tossing it up with some salad, or with some tomato/cuke/lemon/fresh mint/feta to make some homemade couscous. (My husband will eat it, if I call it couscous... but won't if I say it's quinoa. MEN.)

I had to do a bit of online research to make sure I was not ordering something different than what I wanted. But yes, the only thing different about it is the package. top_five_star_obscene.text: I need caffeine to get through the day.  I just do.  Hey, there are worse things.  But like a lot of people these days, I'm trying to eat healthier and no longer want to ingest unhealthy colas or spend mega dollars to buy "healthier" bottled or canned alternatives to get a good amount of caffeine in the form of a cold drink.  I looked at and tried other options, but True Lemon Energy is the best overall I've found to date.  The combination of convenience, zero calories, 120mg of caffeine (from green tea), with no artificial flavors or preservatives was just about everything I was looking for (and it's non-GMO).  So is it perfect?  Of course not.  What is?  It's sweetened with Stevia, which to me has a slight aftertaste, but it's still a helluva lot better than other no-calorie, artificial sweeteners out there so I cut it some slack here.  It's also overly sweet to me as I don't have much of a sweet tooth, so I add a couple packets of True Lemon's lemon packets (just pure lemon crystals -- also excellent in tea or plain seltzer as a cheaper and easier alternative to real lemons BTW) to cut the sweetness a bit and it's much better.  They say to add the Energy powder to 16 ounces of water, but I use 26 ounces of filtered water and I find that to work better (and also gets me drinking more water).  The powder isn't the easiest to get to mix thoroughly in cold water -- probably because they don't add any artificial crap to help it do so -- so I bought a 28-ounce Blender Bottle Sportmixer that mixes it perfectly with just a few shakes.  The powder is still doable in a regular cup as long as you add the water first, but I find the Blender Bottle easier and much better at getting all the powder to dissolve completely.  At first I thought these powder tubes were a little pricey at 62 cents per tube when you buy a four pack (24 tubes total) here on Amazon, but given the lack of good alternatives and that True Lemon uses relatively good and healthier ingredients justifies its price to me.  When I think of all the crap I'm no longer putting in my body and the amount of money I'm saving over buying healthy alternatives at the store, I'm really happy with this True Lemon Energy powder.  And my kids like the regular True Lemon powders without the caffeine, and they're so much better than artificial fruit juice mixes and a nice alternative to real fruit juice/boxes with all their calories.  So for all the reasons listed, TL Energy powder gets a high recommendation from me.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Awesome alternative to other caffeinated drinks

top_five_star_obscene.text: I hosted a Moscow Mule party last year.  During that party, we tried several different brands of ginger beer (Fever Tree, Gosling's, Sioux City, and Cock 'n Bull).  Of those four brands, Cock 'n Bull was the unanimous choice of all of my guests at the party.  That vote includes drinking it straight and drinking it as a mixer in the Moscow Mule.  It has a distinct ginger punch that is lacking in the other brands (a punch that is needed to stand up to the fresh lime juice in the Moscow Mule), yet it is palatable enough to be served on its own.  This is a great mixer, and a great soda.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Best, hands down

top_five_star_obscene.text:
After being burned several times with online floral delivery companies that use local florists and allow 'substitutions' I had pretty much given up on using online flower deliveries. notably a 3-letter abbreviated company allowing a 50$ rose bouquet substitution of 50$ worth of carnations and lillies because the local florist had 'run out of roses' in the middle of January. Both calling said company and being denied any help and complaining directly to the florist involved (hometown flowershop that I had already decided to try not to use over similar nonsense in the past!!) I had told myself -nope. If I can't pick the flowers myself from a display case, I just won't buy any. Then, of course, valentines rolls around and my widowed mother expresses sadness that she never gets roses anymore and here I am overseas almost a full 24 hour time zone removed - with only days to go until the big day. Yikes, right? So I scrapped my other gift plans, intending to pay out the nose for the aforementioned delivery company to go through my hometown florist who would undoubtedly use that larger company's atrocious substitution policy to pawn off whatever stock they wanted to move by claiming roses were 'out of stock'. Instead at the 11th hour a friend mentioned to look on Amazon. Lo and behold... of course I was anxious, I figured my mom would get crappy flowers, but at least I knew they'd be crappy roses, right?

Wrong. They were amazing roses, arriving as tight buds that slowly bloomed over the course of the week and lasted my mother almost 2 weeks from delivery to when she gave up on them. Granted, mom is religious about trimming stems and changing water, but the flowers were gorgeous and truly seemed like a higher quality than anything we'd gotten from our local florist or the other online delivery company. I'll happily order flowers this way again - just wish they had a few more options on colors and sizes.
